The founder can click "Claim" to have the inquiry also appear in their own chef inbox (creates a duplicate inquiry under their tenant, same as the open booking flow does for matched chefs).

title: Spec: Android Home Screen Widgets
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.estimatedComplexity: large (9+ files, native Kotlin + 1 new backend feature)
metadata.howItWorks: - Tap the `+` input field. Keyboard opens. Type or dictate. Hit enter. Note appears in the list
metadata.backendNeeded: New `chef_quick_notes` table (id, chef_id, text, created_at, status: raw|triaged|dismissed). New server action `addQuickNote()`. New API endpoint `POST /api/v2/quick-notes`. Dashboard section to display and triage notes.
metadata.whyThisIs1: The developer said "I'm constantly taking notes down all day. Literally all day." This is the capture mechanism. Everything else in ChefFlow organizes and acts on information. This is how it gets in.
metadata.data: Inquiries, client messages (conversations), Gmail threads, Remy conversations. Merged and sorted by recency.
metadata.shows: The next upcoming event's prep list (or the currently active one if an event is in_progress).
metadata.dashboardSectionFIRSTCLASSFEATURENotJust: Quick Notes lives on the chef portal dashboard as a prominent, always-visible section. Not a collapsible card buried at the bottom. This is where the chef's raw stream of consciousness lands, and where they triage it into real actions.
metadata.remyIntegration: Remy can read raw brain dump notes and suggest triage actions. "You wrote 'email Johnson back' 3 hours ago. Want me to draft a reply?" This uses existing Remy action patterns, not new AI features.
metadata.phaseABackend1Session): 1. Quick Notes table + server actions + dashboard section
metadata.phaseBAndroidNative12Sessions): 1. WidgetDataFetcher (shared HTTP client) + WidgetPreferences (encrypted key storage)

title: Spec: API Integration Reinstatement and Health Audit
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.updated: 2026-03-27 (v3 - blast radius analysis, caller files listed, Maps diagnosis corrected, dead code confirmed)
metadata.nOTCovered: Edamam, USDA, Geocodio, API Ninjas, OCR.space, Yelp, Twilio, OneSignal, Tavily, Frankfurter, Nager.Date.
metadata.chainFormat: `Env Var` -> `Library Module` -> `Server Action / API Route` -> `UI Component / Page`
metadata.test: If key set, `POST https://api.tavily.com/search` with `{"api_key":"{key}","query":"test","max_results":1}` - verify 200.
metadata.silentFailure: Returns `null` to caller. Console logs the error but UI has no way to see it. Chef sees "No receipt found" when OCR service was down.
metadata.rateLimit: 150 requests/day (shared across all three use cases: pricing, wine, nutrition). Check remaining quota in response headers.
metadata.possiblyOrphaned: `getWinePairing()`, `getDishPairing()`, `getWineDescription()` are exported from `spoonacular-wine.ts` but the research could not confirm any UI component calling them. Builder must definitively trace callers: `grep` for imports of these functions. If no callers, flag as dead code in the report (do not delete without developer approval).
metadata.dEMOKEYIssue: Without a real key, `usda-client.ts` and `usda-fdc.ts` fall back to `DEMO_KEY` (30 req/hour vs 1,000 req/hour with a real key). Free key available at https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/api-key-signup.html.
metadata.silentFailureDANGEROUS): `analyzeRecipe()` returns `null` on API failure. Caller shows zero allergens. A recipe containing peanuts could show a clean allergen badge because the API was down. This is a safety issue, not just a UX issue.
metadata.envVarInconsistencyMUSTFIX): `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER` and `TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER` are used in different files for what may be the same number. `lib/sms/send.ts` reads `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER`. `lib/sms/twilio-client.ts` reads `TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER`. `app/api/comms/sms/route.ts` reads `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER`. If the developer sets only one, half the SMS code silently fails.
metadata.fix: Pick one canonical name. Add an alias in the other file (`const from = process.env.TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER || process.env.TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER`). Update `.env.local.example` to document one name.
metadata.cRITICAL: All three Stripe keys are blank in `.env.local`. This means all payment functionality is non-functional. Developer must provide keys. Note: these are empty strings (`""`), not undefined. The health check must test for this specifically because `""` is falsy in `if (key)` but NOT undefined in `if (key !== undefined)`.
metadata.note: Yelp is the ONE integration with proper error handling: it throws on HTTP failure and persists errors to the `last_error` DB field. Use this as the reference pattern when fixing other integrations.
metadata.cRITICALDependency: `SOCIAL_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is required for storing tokens. Without it, any social platform OAuth connection will **crash** on token save. If any social platform env vars are configured but this key is not, the health check must flag it as CRITICAL.
metadata.iMPORTANT: Without the secret key, Turnstile verification is bypassed in dev (acceptable). In production, forms are unprotected from bots. The health check should flag this as WARN in dev, CRITICAL in production.
metadata.theCallerThenDecides: show error state (toast, inline message, "could not check") instead of pretending data doesn't exist.
metadata.nOTE: Additional callers of `resolvePublicLocationQuery()` may exist in `lib/discovery/actions.ts` and `lib/directory/location-search.ts`. Builder must grep to confirm and update all callers.
metadata.whyTheseAreSeparate: The return type stays `Alert[]` / `Estimate[]`. The fix is adding a synthetic alert/estimate entry when the underlying service is down, so Remy's message is honest. No callers need updating.

title: Spec: Auto-Costing Engine
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.whyBlocking: If `ingredient_price_history` has no rows where `source LIKE 'openclaw_%'`, the entire resolution chain below Tier 2 is empty and auto-costing has nothing to work with.
metadata.ifBroken: Fix the sync's price normalization before proceeding. Every number in the auto-costing engine depends on this being correct.
metadata.whatToSeed: At minimum 500 common ingredients covering all categories (protein, produce, dairy, pantry, spice, oil, baking, frozen, canned, fresh_herb, dry_herb, condiment, beverage, specialty). Each needs:
metadata.dataSourcesUseAllThreeInThisPriorityOrde: 1. **`scripts/openclaw-scraped-prices.json`** (988KB, already on disk) - parse this file to extract unique ingredient names. These are real product names from local stores (Market Basket, Walmart, Hannaford, etc.). Extract the canonical ingredient name from each product (e.g., "Market Basket Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast 2.5lb" -> "Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast"). This gives you 300-500 real ingredient names that match what OpenClaw already prices.
metadata.delivery: Migration file `database/migrations/20260401000113_seed_system_ingredients.sql` with INSERT statements. The table uses RLS (only service_role can write), so the migration runs as superuser.
metadata.minimumViableSeed: 500 ingredients. Must cover every `ingredient_category` enum value. At least 50% should have `weight_to_volume_ratio` populated (the rest can be null; the conversion engine will fall back to `COMMON_DENSITIES` or skip cross-type conversion). Every ingredient must have `name`, `category`, `unit_type`, `standard_unit`, and `allergen_tags` populated.
metadata.theProblem: Two code paths compute recipe costs with different capabilities:
metadata.recommendationThisSpecAdoptsThis): - **TypeScript is the authority** for computing and storing costs. It has unit conversion, which SQL doesn't.
metadata.tasks: 1. Add cost refresh button to costing page

title: Spec: Chef-Facing Price Catalog - Full Upgrade
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.boundaryNote20260401): This chef-facing catalog can still exist as a product outcome, but visible chef-facing copy must describe it as ChefFlow's market or price catalog, not as OpenClaw. OpenClaw naming belongs only in internal docs/admin surfaces.
metadata.scopeNote20260401): This spec is the approved authority for a chef-facing ChefFlow market catalog. It supersedes the earlier admin-only catalog assumption inside `openclaw-price-surfacing.md`.
metadata.authNote: These use `requireChef()`, not `requireAdmin()`. The existing admin catalog actions (`searchCatalog`, `getCatalogItemPrices`) use `requireAdmin()` and remain unchanged. Do NOT modify the existing admin actions.
metadata.actionButtonsTopRightOfExpandedPanel): - **"Add to My Pantry"** button: adds this ingredient to the chef's personal ingredient library. Uses `addCatalogIngredientToLibrary()` with the best price pre-filled. Shows toast on success. Disabled if already in library (check by name match).
metadata.perStorePriceTable: | Store | Location | Price | Stock | Type | Source | Confirmed | Link |
metadata.sharedComponent: `components/pricing/freshness-dot.tsx` is used by both specs. Build it once here. The admin catalog spec references it by import path.
metadata.ifBuildingBoth: Do all Pi changes in a single session. Modify `sync-api.mjs` once, SCP once, restart once. Deploy all Pi changes, verify all endpoints, then build both UIs.
metadata.ifBuildingSequentially: This spec goes first (it's chef-facing, higher priority). catalog-power-tools builds on top.
metadata.existingPatternsToFollow: - Server actions calling Pi: copy the abort controller + timeout pattern from `lib/openclaw/catalog-actions.ts`
metadata.infiniteScrollCursorImplementation: - The `after` cursor is the `ingredient_id` of the last item in the current result set.
metadata.nonBlockingPiCalls: All Pi calls must be wrapped in try/catch with AbortSignal.timeout(). If the Pi is offline, show a clear error state. Never show an empty catalog as if there are zero ingredients.
metadata.navPlacement: Add under Culinary section in `nav-config.tsx`. Use the `Store` icon from lucide. Set `tier: 'secondary'`. Do NOT set `adminOnly: true`. Place it after "Ingredients" and before "Costing" in the Culinary group.
metadata.buildOrder: 1. Pi changes: add composite index to `db.mjs`, add `/api/categories`, update `/api/ingredients` (stock counts, cursor, filters), add `/api/ingredients/detail/:id`

title: Spec: Catalog Power Tools - 9 Enhancements to Admin Price Catalog
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.what: Push filter state to the URL as query params so filtered views are bookmarkable and shareable.
metadata.behavior: - Category badge in each table row: `onClick` calls `handleCategoryClick(item.category)` (already exists)
metadata.uIInCatalogTabTsx: - In the Best Price column, after the price text, render a trend indicator:
metadata.rendering: - Horizontal stacked bars, one per category
metadata.dataSource: The Pi already tracks price changes. The `getCatalogItemPrices()` action returns prices but not trends. Instead of adding a new endpoint, derive the trend client-side from the expanded price data, or add a lightweight field to the search results.
metadata.approachAPreferredNoPiChange): Add trend data to the `/api/ingredients` response on the Pi.
metadata.performanceNote: This window function runs across all `price_changes` rows from the last 7 days. If the query adds >500ms latency to `/api/ingredients`, make trends opt-in via a `?trends=1` query param (only sent when the user hasn't disabled it). Measure before and after on the Pi.
metadata.visualFeedback: - Focused row gets `ring-1 ring-brand-500 ring-inset` highlight
metadata.accessibility: - Add `tabIndex={0}` to the table wrapper so it can receive focus
metadata.uRLParams: | Param    | Maps to         | Default (omitted from URL) |
metadata.readOnMount: Initialize state from URL params instead of hardcoded defaults. If `?cat=beef&store=123` is in the URL, start with those filters active.
metadata.writeOnChange: Every filter change calls `router.replace(pathname + '?' + newParams.toString(), { scroll: false })` in addition to triggering `doSearch()`. Use `replace` not `push` to avoid polluting browser history with every filter change.
metadata.important: The catalog tab is rendered inside `price-catalog-client.tsx` which manages tabs via local state (no URL params). The URL params must not conflict. Use a `tab` param if the builder also wants to URL-sync tabs, otherwise leave tab management as-is (local state is fine for tabs).
metadata.cRITICALSuspenseBoundaryRequired: `useSearchParams()` in Next.js App Router throws if not wrapped in `<Suspense>`. The parent `price-catalog-client.tsx` currently renders `<CatalogTab />` directly with no Suspense. The builder MUST:
metadata.cSVExportRouteReadsFromURL: The route at `csv-export/route.ts` reads filter params from `request.nextUrl.searchParams`, NOT from a POST body. The client constructs a URL like `/admin/price-catalog/csv-export?cat=beef&priced=1` and calls `window.open(url)` to trigger the download.

metadata.feature2CSVExport: 6. Apply filters (category: beef, priced only) 7. Click Download button 8. Open CSV, verify it contains only beef items with prices 9. Verify no formula injection (no cells starting with `=`)
metadata.feature3BulkPriceChecker:

salmon
butter
za'atar
fake_ingredient_xyz` 12. Click "Check Prices" 13. Verify found items show prices, fake item shows "not found" 14. Verify total row at bottom 15. Click an ingredient name, verify it populates the search field
metadata.feature4FreshnessDots: 16. Look at the Last Updated column 17. Verify green dots for recent dates, amber for older, red for very old 18. Hover over a dot, verify tooltip text
metadata.feature5ClickToFilter: 19. Click a "beef" category badge in the results table 20. Verify the category filter activates and results update 21. Clear filters, expand an item, click a store name 22. Verify the store filter activates
metadata.feature6CategoryCoverage: 23. Check the stats bar, verify "Show breakdown" toggle exists 24. Click it, verify per-category bars appear 25. Verify worst-coverage categories are at the top 26. Click a category bar, verify it filters the main table

title: Chef User Journey Interrogation
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.q1LandingPageToSignupForm: What does a prospective chef see at `cheflowhq.com` that tells them what ChefFlow does? Is the value proposition clear in under 5 seconds? Does the CTA lead directly to `/auth/signup`?
metadata.q2SignupFormFields: `signUpChef()` in `lib/auth/actions.ts:156` requires email + password. `business_name` and `phone` are optional. Does the signup UI collect enough to personalize the first session, or does the chef land in a generic void?
metadata.q3SignupAtomicity: If Stripe customer creation fails (line 253), the chef record still exists but has no subscription. What state is the chef in? Can they use the app? Does any UI surface "your account setup is incomplete"?
metadata.q4GoogleOAuthSignup: A chef clicks "Sign in with Google" but has no existing account. Does the OAuth callback create a chef record + role + preferences atomically? Or does it only create an `auth.users` record, leaving the chef in limbo with no `chefs` row?
metadata.q5DuplicateEmailHandling: `signUpChef()` throws a generic error for existing emails (line 170). Good for security. But does the signin page offer a "Forgot password?" flow? If a chef can't remember whether they signed up, can they recover without contacting support?
metadata.q6PostLoginLanding: After signup, the chef hits `requireChef()` in `app/(chef)/layout.tsx:76`. Where do they redirect? Dashboard? Onboarding wizard? What's the default state of `onboarding_completed_at` for a new chef?
metadata.q7DashboardWithZeroData: `app/(chef)/dashboard/page.tsx` renders HeroMetrics, ScheduleCards, AlertCards, BusinessCards, IntelligenceCards, SmartSuggestions, MetricsStrip, WeeklyBriefingCard, etc. With zero events, zero clients, zero revenue: does each widget show an empty state, show $0 (hallucination), or crash?
metadata.q8OnboardingWizardCompleteness: `WIZARD_STEPS` has 6 steps (profile, portfolio, first_menu, pricing, connect_gmail, first_booking). Can a chef skip ALL steps and still use the app? What's the minimum viable path to a functional account?
metadata.q9SkipAllBehavior: `handleSkipAll()` in `onboarding-wizard.tsx:64` calls both `dismissOnboardingBanner()` and `completeOnboardingWizard()` then navigates to `/dashboard`. If both fail (DB down), it still navigates. Is this fail-open behavior intentional? Can a chef get trapped?
metadata.q10OnboardingBannerPersistence: If a chef dismisses the onboarding banner, then logs out and back in, does the banner stay dismissed? The check uses `onboarding_banner_dismissed_at` on the chefs table. Is this column populated on dismiss?
metadata.q11EmptySidebarNavigation: The sidebar shows all nav sections regardless of data. A new chef sees Events, Clients, Calendar, Culinary, etc. All clicking through to empty pages. Does this feel like "a tool ready for me" or "a ghost town I don't understand"?
metadata.q12ProfileMinimumViability: What is the absolute minimum a chef needs in their profile to be functional? Business name defaults to email prefix at signup. Is that enough for: sending quotes? Appearing in embed widget? Having clients see their portal?
metadata.q13ProfileCompletenessIndicator: Does any surface tell the chef "your profile is 30% complete" outside the onboarding wizard? If they skip onboarding, is there any nudge to complete their profile later?
metadata.q14PublicProfileExposure: `app/(chef)/settings/public-profile/page.tsx` exists. If a chef enables `isPublic` with zero portfolio items, zero bio, and email-prefix business name, what does the public profile look like? Is it embarrassing?
metadata.q15ServiceTypeSelection: `app/(chef)/settings/my-services/page.tsx` lets chefs define what they offer. If a chef never visits this page, what service types are assumed? Does quoting, inquiry processing, or client-facing content break without service types set?
metadata.q16ClientCreationWithoutAnInquiry: Can a chef manually add a client at `/clients` without receiving an inquiry first? What fields are required? Can they add a client with just a name and phone number (how most chefs actually know their clients)?
metadata.q17EmbedWidgetSetup: `app/(chef)/settings/embed/page.tsx` configures the public inquiry form. What does a chef need to set up before the embed widget works? Does the widget function with a bare-minimum profile?
metadata.q18InquiryReceiptAndResponse: A potential client submits an inquiry via the embed widget. What notification does the chef receive? Email? In-app? Push? If the chef doesn't check the app for 48 hours, is the lead cold with no follow-up?
metadata.q19InquiryToEventConversion: When a chef wants to convert an inquiry into an event, what's the flow? How many clicks/steps? Does the inquiry data (date, guest count, dietary needs) pre-populate the event form?
metadata.q20ClientPortalFromDayOne: When a chef adds their first client, does the client automatically get portal access? Or does the chef need to explicitly invite them? What does the client see on first login?
metadata.q21MenuCreationWithoutRecipes: Can a chef create a menu at `/culinary/menus` without having any recipes in the system? Can they type dish names directly into a menu, or must recipes exist first?
metadata.q22RecipeEntryFriction: A chef has 200 recipes in their head. The recipe form: how many fields are required vs optional? Can they batch-enter "Grilled Salmon, Pan-Seared Duck, Risotto" in rapid fire, or does each recipe require a full form submission?
metadata.q23RecipeFromTextPhoto: Does any AI-assisted recipe entry exist? Can a chef paste a block of text (or photograph a handwritten recipe) and have it parsed into structured data? `lib/ai/parse-recipe.ts` exists. Is it wired to the UI?
metadata.q24MenuWithoutPricing: A chef creates a menu with 5 dishes. None have ingredient costs entered. What does the menu cost display show? $0? "Not priced"? A broken layout?
metadata.q25IngredientCatalogForNewUsers: `lib/pricing/resolve-price.ts` has a 10-tier fallback chain. For a brand new chef with zero manual prices and zero OpenClaw data in their region, how many tiers actually resolve? What does the chef see? "No price data available" or a misleading $0?
metadata.q26EventCreationPrerequisites: To create an event at `/events/new`, does the chef need: a client? A menu? A price? Or can they create a bare event with just a date, location, and guest count?
metadata.q27QuoteGenerationWithMissingData: A chef creates an event and wants to send a quote. What happens if: no menu attached? No pricing set? No services defined? Does the quote form gracefully handle missing data, or does it render a $0 quote?
metadata.q28QuoteDeliveryToClient: How does a quote reach the client? Email? Client portal? PDF? What if the chef hasn't set up email (no SMTP, no Gmail connection)? Can they still generate a shareable quote link?
metadata.q29EventStateMachineForNovices: The FSM has 8 states (draft -> proposed -> accepted -> paid -> confirmed -> in_progress -> completed | cancelled). Does a new chef understand what "proposed" means? Is there UI guidance explaining the lifecycle?
metadata.q30PaymentCollection: A client accepts a quote. How does the chef collect payment? Stripe Connect must be set up. If it isn't, what does the chef see? A dead "Collect Payment" button? An error? A prompt to set up Stripe?
metadata.q31CalendarWithFirstEvent: Chef creates their first event for next Saturday. Does the calendar view (`/calendar`) render correctly with a single event? Do day/week/year views all handle the sparse-data case?
metadata.q32ShoppingListGeneration: Chef has an event with a menu. Can they generate a shopping list? What if ingredient quantities aren't specified in recipes? Does the shopping list show items without amounts?
metadata.q33CallSheetGeneration: `/culinary/call-sheet` exists. For a chef's first event, what does the call sheet contain? Is it useful or empty scaffolding?
metadata.q34DayOfExecution: On event day, the chef opens the app. What does the dashboard show? Is there a "today's event" hero card? Can they see the timeline, menu, client dietary notes, and shopping list from one place?
metadata.q35EventCompletion: After the event, how does the chef mark it complete? What's the path from `in_progress` to `completed`? Does the app prompt for post-event actions (send thank you, request review, generate AAR)?
metadata.q36RevenueVisibilityFromZero: A chef completes their first event and receives $2,000. Where do they see their total revenue? Dashboard hero metrics? A dedicated financial page? Does the number update immediately or require a page refresh?
metadata.q37ExpenseTracking: Can a chef log expenses (groceries, supplies, travel) against an event? Where? Is food cost percentage calculated automatically?
metadata.q38ProfitCalculationHonesty: If a chef has revenue but zero logged expenses, does the app show 100% profit margin? That's technically true but misleading. Does it distinguish "no expenses logged" from "no expenses incurred"?
metadata.q39FinancialReportsForTaxPrep: A chef needs to file taxes. Can they export revenue/expense data by date range? CSV? PDF? Or is this a "coming soon" feature?
metadata.q40MultiplePaymentMethods: Client pays $1,000 deposit via Stripe and $1,000 cash on event day. Can the system track split payments? Does the ledger handle mixed payment types?
metadata.q41RebookingAPastClient: A client from 3 months ago wants to book again. What's the fastest path? Can the chef find the client, see past event history, and create a new event pre-populated with their preferences?
metadata.q42ClientDietaryMemory: The system stores dietary restrictions. When a repeat client books, do their allergies/restrictions auto-populate on the new event? Or does the chef re-enter them every time?
metadata.q43RecurringServiceSetup: A client wants weekly meal prep. Can the chef set up a recurring event/schedule? Or must they create 52 individual events?
metadata.q44ClientCommunicationHistory: Can the chef see all past messages, emails, and interactions with a specific client in one place? Or is communication scattered across email, the messaging tab, and event notes?
metadata.q45ReferralTracking: Client A refers Client B. Can the chef track this relationship? Does it affect loyalty scoring? Is there any attribution from inquiry source to referral?
metadata.q46ConcurrentEventManagement: A chef has 5 events this week. Can they see all of them at a glance with their status, preparation progress, and outstanding tasks? Where?
metadata.q47StaffAssignment: Chef hires a sous chef for a large event. `/staff` page exists. Can they assign staff to specific events? What's the staff member's experience (do they get their own login)?
metadata.q48EquipmentTracking: `app/(chef)/settings` has equipment inventory. Is this connected to events? Can a chef see "I need my 20-quart stock pot for Saturday's event" or is it just an inventory list?
metadata.q49MultiMenuEvents: A wedding with cocktail hour, dinner, and late-night snacks. Can a chef attach multiple menus to one event? How does costing work across menus?
metadata.q50SeasonalPricingAwareness: A chef quotes a December event with lobster. Lobster prices spike in December. Does the pricing engine account for seasonality, or does it quote April prices for December events?
metadata.q51OfflineBehavior: Chef is at a farmer's market with spotty reception. What happens when they try to use the app? PWA is disabled by default. Does the app degrade gracefully or show a white screen?
metadata.q52MobileExperience: Chef opens the app on their phone (most common use case). Is the full app responsive? Are critical flows (check today's schedule, view client details, mark event in progress) usable on a 375px screen?
metadata.q53DataRecovery: Chef accidentally deletes a client. Is there an undo? Soft delete? Or is the data gone permanently?
metadata.q54SessionTimeout: Chef is mid-quote-creation and their session expires. What happens? Is their draft saved? Do they lose 20 minutes of work?
metadata.q55ConcurrentAccess: Chef and their business partner both log in to the same account. SSE realtime is in place. Do they see each other's changes in real time? Or does one overwrite the other?
metadata.q56BulkClientImport: A 10-year veteran has 300 clients in a spreadsheet. Is there a CSV import? Bulk add? Or must they enter 300 clients one by one?
metadata.q57HistoricalEventBackfill: Veteran wants to see their business trajectory. Can they enter past events with dates, revenue, and client info? Does the financial dashboard properly handle historical data?
metadata.q58RecipeLibraryMigration: Chef has 200 recipes in various formats (Google Docs, photos, handwritten notes). Beyond `parse-recipe.ts`, is there a bulk import flow? Can they upload a folder of recipe files?
metadata.q59ExistingClientRelationships: Veteran's clients already have dietary preferences, event history, and communication patterns. Where does this institutional knowledge go? Is there a client notes field? A relationship timeline?
metadata.q60BusinessAnalyticsFromDayOne: A veteran joining ChefFlow wants to see their business health immediately, not after 6 months of data collection. Can the system provide value from imported historical data, or does everything require fresh events?

title: Commerce & Financial Pipeline Integrity Interrogation
summary: **Purpose:** Expose every failure point in ChefFlow's money layer: POS checkout, payments, refunds, ledger, reconciliation, tax, reporting, and export. Every chef archetype depends on financial accuracy. A bug here = real money lost, incorrect tax filings, or broken trust with clients. This interrogation forces the entire commerce-to-ledger pipeline into a fully specified, verifiable state.
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.purpose: Expose every failure point in ChefFlow's money layer: POS checkout, payments, refunds, ledger, reconciliation, tax, reporting, and export. Every chef archetype depends on financial accuracy. A bug here = real money lost, incorrect tax filings, or broken trust with clients. This interrogation forces the entire commerce-to-ledger pipeline into a fully specified, verifiable state.
metadata.scope: The full money flow: checkout -> payment -> ledger -> reconciliation -> reporting -> export. Covers POS register operations, card/cash handling, split tenders, refunds, tips, tax, daily close-out, period reporting, CPA-ready export, and cross-system consistency.
metadata.principle: ChefFlow has a sophisticated commerce engine (checkout, split tender, promotions, terminal adapter, audit log, observability). But sophistication without integrity is a liability. This interrogation tests whether the numbers are CORRECT, CONSISTENT, and RECOVERABLE at every stage.
metadata.question: Can any server action delete financial records (ledger entries, payments, sales in non-draft status, refunds)?
metadata.whyItMatters: Deleting financial records is illegal in most jurisdictions (tax audit trail requirements). The system must prevent it at the code level.
metadata.passCriteria: No exported server action deletes from `ledger_entries`, `commerce_payments`, `commerce_refunds`, or `sales` (except draft->void which is a status change, not deletion). DB triggers enforce immutability.
metadata.byPriority: - P0 (12 questions): 10 PASS, 2 PARTIAL (FQ9, FQ25), 0 FAIL

metadata.iMPORTANTFIX: `removeStaffFromEvent` deletes by `assignmentId`, but `removeStaffFromCrewCircle` needs `staffMemberId`. The assignment row is already deleted by the time we call this. Solution: query the assignment's `staff_member_id` BEFORE the delete, store it in a local variable, then pass it to `removeStaffFromCrewCircle`. This requires moving the query before the delete.
metadata.onlyChangeNeeded: In the circle dashboard UI (`app/(chef)/circles/page.tsx`), add a visual indicator (badge or label) when `group_type === 'crew'` so the chef can distinguish crew circles from client circles. This is a small conditional in the circle list item rendering.

title: Spec: David's Docket (OpenClaw Cartridge)
classificationReason: Blocked by hard-stop language or source status.

metadata.dependsOn: Existing OpenClaw dashboard at Pi:8090 (SSH resolved 2026-04-05)
metadata.estimatedComplexity: large (9+ files across Pi and PC)
metadata.listView: Shows all docket items with columns: title, status, priority, output type, confidence, processing time, created date. Status badges color-coded:
metadata.actionsPerItem: Edit, Claim, Delete, View Output (when done), Re-process (send back to pending)
metadata.setup: One-time `git clone` of the ChefFlow repo from GitHub. Read-only. Never pushes.
metadata.updateSchedule: `git pull` every hour via cron. Runs before the docket processor so context is fresh.
metadata.whatTheProcessorReadsFromTheMirror: For every docket item, the processor loads context in this order:
metadata.contextBudget: Maximum 30KB of text fed to the model per item. Groq's free tier has a 12K token-per-minute limit; 30KB context + system/user prompt fits within this. Local qwen2.5-coder:7b handles 32K tokens for fallback. If targeted files exceed 30KB, prioritize: project map > app audit section > source files (newest first). CLAUDE.md is loaded selectively (too large for the full file).
metadata.trigger: Cron job every 10 minutes (fast cycle). Also triggerable manually from the dashboard ("Process Now" button). High-priority items trigger immediate processing via a webhook from the dashboard API.
metadata.developerFeedbackLoop: Each completed item shows a thumbs up/down on the dashboard. Feedback is stored in docket.db. If 3+ consecutive items get thumbs down, the processor auto-flags subsequent items as "needs human session" until the developer resets the quality gate. This prevents wasting processing on bad prompts or model drift.
metadata.outputDocumentFormat: Every output document includes a header block that identifies it as docket-produced:
metadata.schedule: Runs as part of the existing hourly sync, or on-demand via the Operator dashboard.
metadata.pCSideChanges: - `scripts/openclaw-pull/sync-all.mjs` - Added Step 4: pull docket output docs, mark as pulled on Pi
metadata.aIRouting: - Primary: Groq Llama 3.3 70B (2-second processing, free tier)
metadata.e2ETestResult: Item submitted, processed by Groq in 2 seconds, output saved with medium confidence, correct frontmatter and structure.

title: Dinner Circle Cohesion Interrogation: 82-Question Audit
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.scope: `components/hub/theme-picker.tsx`, `lib/hub/group-actions.ts` theme storage.
metadata.method: Code-level investigation of all 82 questions, graded against real implementation
metadata.principle: The Dinner Circle is ChefFlow's center of gravity. ALL user types converge here. Every question must be answerable with binary pass/fail.
metadata.hypothesis: When a circle owner sets a visual theme (colors, emoji), the theme persists across sessions and is visible to all members. Theme is stored in the group record, not in localStorage.
metadata.failure: Theme resets on page reload, or only the setter sees it (localStorage-only), or it applies to the wrong circle.
metadata.impact: LOW. Profile tokens are already in localStorage and URL fragments. But URL query params are logged more aggressively than fragments.
metadata.fixDirection: Use `POST` form submission instead of `GET` query param, or pass token via `sessionStorage` before redirect.

metadata.dependsOn: none (builds on existing hub_groups + chef network infra)
metadata.prerequisite: An accepted `chef_connections` row must exist between the two chefs ([chef_friends_network.sql:36-60](database/migrations/20260221000002_chef_friends_network.sql#L36-L60)). The `assertConnected()` helper in [collaboration/actions.ts:38-54](lib/collaboration/actions.ts#L38-L54) validates this.
metadata.flow: 1. Chef A navigates to `/network` and clicks "Create Circle" on Chef B's connection card
metadata.multiChefCircles: A chef-chef circle can have more than 2 members. Any `chef`-role member can invite another connected chef. The connection gate applies per invite (inviter must be connected to invitee).
metadata.currentState: `event_collaborators` ([collaboration_system.sql:17-59](database/migrations/20260304000008_chef_collaboration_system.sql#L17-L59)) manages cross-chef event access with roles and JSONB permissions. `inviteChefToEvent()` ([collaboration/actions.ts:122-236](lib/collaboration/actions.ts#L122-L236)) handles invitations. No shared communication channel exists.
metadata.newBehavior: When Chef A invites Chef B (a circle member) to collaborate on an event:
metadata.privacy: When a chef leaves the circle, their `hub_circle_shared_recipes` rows are soft-deleted (revoked). The recipe cards in the feed show "Recipe no longer shared" instead of content. Already-imported copies are unaffected (they are independent deep copies).
metadata.clientConsent: When a chef shares a client who is an existing `clients` row in their tenant, a consent flag is recorded. The client receives an email: "Chef A shared your contact info with Chef B for [occasion]. If this was not expected, contact Chef A." This is informational, not blocking. The chef owns the referral relationship.
metadata.financialSplit: Per the existing `event_collaborators.permissions.can_view_financials` flag. Revenue split details are stored in `event_collaborators.permissions` as additional fields (`revenue_split_pct`). The ledger stays single-tenant; split is a display-layer calculation, not a ledger mutation.
metadata.compatibilityWithTicketedEvents: The `event_collab` circle is compatible with [ticketed-events-and-distribution.md](docs/specs/ticketed-events-and-distribution.md). The co-host circle admin bridge described in Phase 3 of that spec maps directly to the `chef` role in the `event_collab` circle. No conflict.
metadata.implementation: This is a structured message type in `hub_messages`, not a new table. The `system_metadata` JSONB field holds: `{ type: 'co_purchase', item, quantity, unit, unit_price_cents, supplier, deadline, responses: [{ chef_id, quantity }] }`.
metadata.a: This spec was directly requested by the developer based on a real use case ("I have a chef friend with his own business"). It is driven by validated personal need, not speculative feature expansion.

title: Dinner Lifecycle End-to-End Interrogation
summary: **Purpose:** Close every gap between how a chef actually runs a dinner and what ChefFlow supports. Each question targets a specific friction point where a real chef would leave ChefFlow and open a different app. The system passes when a chef NEVER needs to leave.
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.passed: Client creation (22s save, redirected to client detail). Event creation linked to client (30s save with scheduling conflict handling, redirected to event detail). 3 recipes created with ingredients (Sage Butter Ravioli, Herb Roasted Chicken, Lemon Olive Oil Tart - each 42s save). Event detail page loads and shows the created event.
metadata.notTested: Menu creation, recipe-to-menu linking via components/dishes, menu-to-event linking, shopping list generation, ingredient consolidation, guest-count scaling, price resolution. The chain is proven from client through recipes but the menu->shopping list segment is untested.
metadata.tested: Propose to Client" button found and clicked (draft state). "Mark Paid (Offline)" button found and clicked (draft state, valid shortcut per FSM rules: draft->paid).
metadata.rootCauseInvestigated: DB query confirmed the FSM mechanism WORKS. An earlier test run (event `0c271e6e`) successfully transitioned `draft->proposed` with a logged `event_state_transitions` row. The latest run's event (`8106d7d3`) has NO transition log beyond initial creation, meaning the button click never reached the server action. Cause: test script timing/interaction issue on slow hardware, NOT a product bug. The `transition_event_atomic` RPC with CAS guard, the `proposeEvent` server action, and the UI button all function correctly.
metadata.notReached: accepted, confirmed, in_progress, completed transitions. No ledger entries verified. Full 6-step traversal needs a dedicated test with 60s+ waits per transition on this hardware.
metadata.aCalendar: Page loads at `/calendar`. Event was created with a date 7 days from test run (April 24). Calendar rendered but event was not visible in the current view - likely requires navigating to the correct month/week. Calendar page itself is functional.
metadata.bDashboard: `/dashboard` loads. Event count and metrics render. Could not isolate whether the specific test event is included in aggregate counts (no per-event breakdown on dashboard).
metadata.cRevenue: Not verified - event payment (via "Mark Paid") did not persist (blocked by S7 FSM issue).
metadata.purpose: Close every gap between how a chef actually runs a dinner and what ChefFlow supports. Each question targets a specific friction point where a real chef would leave ChefFlow and open a different app. The system passes when a chef NEVER needs to leave.
metadata.groundedIn: Real dinner completed 2026-04-16. Developer (10+ year private chef) provided full lifecycle data. System audit performed 2026-04-17 against live codebase.
metadata.method: Developer answers from real experience. Agent verifies against running app via Playwright. Gaps become build tickets. Everything built benefits ALL users.
metadata.whatSNOTConfirmed: Whether the UI surfaces all of this, and whether the workflow between these features is seamless.
metadata.blockingIssue: S7 full traversal (6/6 transitions) not yet tested due to test script timing on slow hardware. FSM mechanism itself is sound (DB-verified). Full traversal test needs 60s+ waits per transition.
metadata.chainStatus: Client -> Event -> Recipe creation works end-to-end. Menu linking is the missing bridge to shopping lists and full cost chain.
metadata.whatTheChefDid: $125/head was agreed by the GROUP (all 6 women), not just the host. But the host collected and paid.
metadata.whatTheSystemNeeds: The quote/payment model assumes one payer. Support: (a) per-head pricing visible to guests (already in quotes), (b) recording who actually paid (host? split?), (c) optional: share payment link with guests.
metadata.benefitToAllUsers: Group dinners are extremely common. The host shouldn't have to be the accountant.

title: Spec: Directory Operator Outreach Pipeline

metadata.buildNotes: Migration uses 000120 (spec said 000117, which was already taken). Spec said modify `outreach.ts` but campaign logic is in separate `outreach-campaign.ts` to keep existing transactional emails untouched. `sendEmail` in `lib/email/send.ts` now accepts optional `from` parameter for sender identity override.
metadata.senderIdentity: A neutral domain dedicated to the directory outreach.
metadata.prioritizationOrderForWhoGetsEmailedFirs: 1. Listings with `lead_score >= 70` AND `email IS NOT NULL` AND `website_url IS NOT NULL` (highest quality, most likely to engage positively)
metadata.batchModel: - Each daily run creates one `outreach_batches` row
metadata.subjectLineVariantsABTestable): - "Want to be featured in [city]'s food directory?
metadata.emailBodyKeyPrinciples): - NO mention of ChefFlow, cheflowhq.com, or any existing listing
metadata.physicalAddressRendering: The template reads `process.env.OUTREACH_PHYSICAL_ADDRESS` and renders it as a plain text line at the bottom of the email. If the env var is empty/missing, the `sendDirectoryInvitationEmail` function must skip sending and log an error (CAN-SPAM hard requirement).
metadata.pageContent: - Clean, simple form: Business Name, Your Email, City, State, Business Type
metadata.importantUXConstraint: The join page should stay minimal because the outreach email promises a short flow. The heavy lifting belongs after claim. The operator should not be asked to fully rebuild their profile before they can proceed.
metadata.matchingLogicOnSubmitExactAlgorithmInOrd: 1. If `ref` param decrypts to a valid listing UUID: claim that listing directly
metadata.sourceFieldConstraint: If Tier 3 creates a new listing, it must reuse an already-valid `directory_listings.source` value such as `submission` unless a separate migration explicitly expands the source check. Do not invent `outreach_join` under the current schema contract.

title: Spec: Discover Directory Polish
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.current: Landing view has a `Suspense` with skeleton fallback (lines 256-266). But the filtered results branch (lines 267-330) renders synchronously after the `await getDirectoryListings(filters)` call on line 209. There's no loading skeleton because the entire page is server-rendered and the data fetch blocks the render.
metadata.change: Wrap the filtered results section in a `Suspense` with a skeleton fallback. Move the `getDirectoryListings` call into an async child component (like `StateGrid` pattern) so the Suspense boundary can catch the pending promise.
metadata.evidence: `getStateName` appears only on line 11 of discover-filters.tsx. Grep confirms zero usages in the file body.
metadata.analysis: Since this is a server component and the data fetch happens before render, a client-side skeleton wouldn't help. The real solution is to wrap the filtered results in a `Suspense` boundary so Next.js can stream the shell first and fill in results after the query completes.

title: Event Financial Lifecycle - Interrogation Question Set
summary: **System under test:** Event FSM (8 states) -> Ledger (append-only) -> Financial Views -> Payment Flows (Stripe + offline) -> Cancellation/Refund -> P&L
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.purpose: Expose every failure point in the event state machine, ledger, payment, and financial reporting pipeline.
metadata.systemUnderTest: Event FSM (8 states) -> Ledger (append-only) -> Financial Views -> Payment Flows (Stripe + offline) -> Cancellation/Refund -> P&L
metadata.theQuestion: Quote is sent with `valid_until = 2026-04-10`. Client doesn't accept until April 20. But they pay on April 25. Does the system accept the payment?
metadata.currentBehavior: `acceptQuote` (client-actions.ts line 128) checks `valid_until` and throws if expired. So the client can't accept after expiry. But: if the quote was accepted before expiry, and the client delays payment until after expiry, the PaymentIntent is still valid in Stripe. The webhook processes the payment normally.
metadata.rootCause: Expiry is checked at acceptance time, not payment time. Once accepted, the event exists and payment can arrive anytime.
metadata.severity: LOW. This is actually correct business logic: once a quote is accepted, it's a contract. The expiry prevents late acceptance, not late payment. Late payment is the client's prerogative (they've already committed).
metadata.passCriteria: Document that quote expiry gates acceptance only, not payment. Once accepted, the price is locked regardless of when payment arrives.
metadata.status: CORRECT - expiry applies to acceptance, not payment.

metadata.bug: Events without a quoted price or deposit display "$0.00" instead of "Not set" or a dash.
metadata.fix: Add `signed_contract` to the `confirmed` blockers list, but as a **soft** gate (warning, not hard block). Many private chef events proceed without formal contracts. Make it hard-blockable via a chef preference toggle (`require_contract_before_confirm`).
metadata.consumersAffected: `getEventFinancialSummary` (page.tsx:191-207), `getEventCloseOutData`, `getMonthlyFinancialSummary`, `computeProfitAndLoss`, client-facing financial summary. The money tab's primary profit summary (`getEventProfitSummary`) is immune because it queries ledger and expenses separately.
metadata.verify: Find or create an event with a recorded tip + at least one payment. Check close-out wizard financial display. Tip should appear once, not inflated.
metadata.alsoCheck: `lib/events/financial-summary-actions.ts` around line 173-177 where `foodCostPercent` and `grossMarginPercent` default to 0 when `actualRevenueCents` is 0. These should be `null` and display as "N/A" in the UI, not "0.0%".
metadata.problem: 25+ non-blocking side effects fire inline during `confirmed` transition (transitions.ts:726-1317). Slow PDF generation could cause timeout.
metadata.add: An input field at the bottom of the pack list (similar to `components/grocery/grocery-list-view.tsx` lines 424-463). Custom items stored in localStorage per event (key: `cf-pack-custom-${eventId}`). Render them in a "Custom Items" section at the end of the checklist.
metadata.doNOTChange: Client-facing social sharing text like "My private chef dinner is officially booked" (that's natural language for social media). Journey step labels shown to clients can keep "dinner" where it reads naturally.
metadata.atMinimum: Apply the spice/herb sublinear scaling rule (converge toward 75% of linear above a threshold). This prevents over-buying dried herbs and spices for large events.
metadata.enhancement: During `in_progress`, add a quick "86 Item" button per dish on the ops tab. When fired:
metadata.decisionNeeded: This is a DB column rename which requires a migration. The column is referenced in:
metadata.optionASafe): Keep the column names, change only UI labels from "Reset" to "Breakdown" in the time tracking component.
metadata.optionBThorough): Additive migration: add `time_breakdown_minutes`, `breakdown_started_at`, `breakdown_completed_at` columns. Backfill from reset columns. Update all references. Drop old columns in a future migration after verification.
metadata.futureFix: Move heavy side effects (PDF generation, email sends with attachments) to a background job queue (Inngest is already used for post-event follow-up). This is architectural and should be a separate spec.

title: Financial Models Remediation - Build Plan
summary: Domain 3 audit found 5 critical defects and 3 moderate defects in ChefFlow's financial layer. The ledger architecture is sound (append-only, integer cents, immutable triggers). The problems are:
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.problem: Line 40: `revenue: e.quoted_price_cents ?? 0` labels quoted price as "revenue". Line 64-65: summary card says "Total revenue" for sum of all quoted prices. Quoted price is invoice value, not revenue. An instructor would say "you can't call an unpaid invoice revenue.
metadata.action: Rename labels only (not the data source, since this page intentionally shows invoice-based profitability).
metadata.validation: Visual check that labels now say "invoiced" not "revenue".
metadata.decision: Standardize on the textbook formula: `food cost / revenue * 100` where revenue = actual collected revenue (not quoted price). Quoted price is invoice value; revenue is cash received. Using quoted price inflates food cost % for events where client paid more (tips, add-ons) and deflates it for underpayments.
metadata.actionB: The DB view's `food_cost_percentage` column returns a 0-1 ratio. The TS gateway in `compute.ts:52` passes it through as-is. Do NOT change the view column or the TS gateway - the ratio is consumed correctly by downstream code that formats it. Document the ratio vs percentage distinction in a code comment at `compute.ts:52`:

title: Spec: Global Loading Feedback Overhaul ("The App Feels Frozen")
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.theProblem: Line 24 has `key={pathname}` on the content wrapper div. This exists to trigger the `animate-fade-slide-up` animation on every navigation. But it has a devastating side effect: React destroys the old content and creates a new empty div that starts at `opacity: 0`. The user sees nothing until the server responds.
metadata.theFix: Remove `key={pathname}`. Keep the old content visible until new content streams in. This is how Next.js App Router is designed to work: the old page stays visible while the new page loads, then swaps seamlessly.
metadata.cleanup: After removing `key={pathname}`, the `usePathname()` import and variable on lines 4 and 11 are now unused. Remove both to avoid lint warnings. The `pathname` is no longer needed in this component.
metadata.verification: Navigate between pages. Old page should remain visible until new page content streams in. No blank screen. If a `loading.tsx` exists for the target page, it should appear as the Suspense fallback while the server component renders.
metadata.noteOnRouterPush`: Click delegation only catches `<a>` link clicks. Programmatic navigation via `router.push()` won't trigger the bar. This is acceptable because Phase 4 adds spinners to those buttons, providing their own feedback. The progress bar covers link-based navigation; button spinners cover programmatic navigation.
metadata.wireIntoLayout: In `app/(chef)/layout.tsx`, add `<NavigationPendingProvider>` inside `<SidebarProvider>` but wrapping everything that needs it (sidebar, mobile nav, and main content). Exact placement:
metadata.wireIntoNavLinks: In `chef-nav.tsx`, wherever nav `<Link>` elements are rendered, add:
metadata.iMPORTANTDISTINCTION: Only replace `disabled={isPending}` with `loading={isPending}` when the button is the one that TRIGGERED the pending action. If a button is disabled because a DIFFERENT action is pending (e.g., a Delete button disabled while a Save is in progress), keep `disabled`. The test: "Is this button's click handler the one that set `isPending` to true?" If yes, use `loading`. If no, keep `disabled`.
metadata.scopeLimit: Fix the top 20 most-used pages in this pass. The rest can be caught incrementally. Do NOT attempt to fix all 500+ buttons at once.
metadata.fixPattern: When a tab triggers data loading, show feedback on the tab and in the content area:
metadata.forTabsThatUseLinkForRoutingNotClientSta: These are already covered by Phase 2 (nav pending state) and Phase 1 (progress bar). No additional work needed.

title: Ingredient Quantity Lifecycle - System Integrity Question Set
summary: **Purpose:** Define the complete set of verifiable questions that, when all answered YES, mean ChefFlow tracks every gram from recipe to leftover with no silent gaps, no dead features, and no conflicting numbers. Every chef benefits.
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.purpose: Define the complete set of verifiable questions that, when all answered YES, mean ChefFlow tracks every gram from recipe to leftover with no silent gaps, no dead features, and no conflicting numbers. Every chef benefits.
metadata.scope: The full ingredient quantity chain: recipe spec, yield adjustment, purchase, usage, leftover, carry-forward, cost reconciliation. Covers both data layer and the workflows that populate it.
metadata.principle: A chef should never have to do math in their head. The system does it, the system is right, and the system can prove it. If a number is shown, it's derived from a real source. If a stage has no data, the system says "not recorded" rather than showing zero.
metadata.hypothesis: When a chef is prepping for an event, they see the RECIPE quantity (what to measure) alongside the BUY quantity (what was purchased), not just one or the other.
metadata.currentState: NOT BUILT as a dedicated view. The recipe card shows recipe quantities. The shopping list shows buy quantities. During prep, the chef needs: "Recipe says measure 2 lbs salmon. You bought 4.4 lbs (whole fish). After filleting you should have ~2 lbs usable.
metadata.failureMode: Chef bought 5 lbs whole salmon (for 2 lbs usable at 45% yield). Looks at recipe: "2 lbs salmon." Looks at their 5 lb fish. "Do I use all of it? Was the 2 lbs before or after filleting?" Confusion during time-critical prep.
metadata.fixRequired: 1. Prep/production view for an event shows per ingredient:
metadata.uXGuidance: Label it "Usable yield %" with helper text: "What percentage is edible after prep? 100 = no waste. 45 = heavy trim (e.g., whole fish to fillet)." Auto-populate from `ingredient_waste_factors` when available.
metadata.verification: Event with a recipe using 2 lbs salmon at 45% yield. Prep view shows: Recipe: 2 lbs | Buy: 4.44 lbs | Yield: 45% | Note: "Expect ~2.44 lbs trim." If purchase was logged (5 lbs), shows: Purchased: 5 lbs | Expected usable: 2.25 lbs (5 \* 0.45).
metadata.dependency: IL1 must pass first. Once yield_pct can be set, this verifies the math works end-to-end.
metadata.phase1UnblockTheDeadSystemAllUsersBenefi: 1. **IL1** - Yield write path. Without this, yield is dead. Everything else depends on it.
metadata.phase2CompleteTheChainHighLeverage): 5. **IL4** - Sub-recipe completeness. Data loss fix, not a new feature. 6. **IL5** - Yield inflation verification. Proves Phase 1 works end-to-end. 7. **IL10** - Unit consistency. Eliminates duplicate line items. 8. **IL12** - Zero vs not-recorded. Trust foundation for all lifecycle data.
metadata.phase3CloseTheLoopWorkflows): 9. **IL6** - Purchase event linkage. Enables Stages 3-5 of the lifecycle. 10. **IL7** - Usage logging workflow. Makes variance analysis real. 11. **IL15** - Under-buy detection. Immediate chef value from IL6. 12. **IL16** - Guest count staleness. Prevents stale saved lists.
metadata.phase4PolishAndPowerFeatures: 13. **IL8** - Leftover carry-forward reduction. Smart inventory usage. 14. **IL20** - Prep view. The payoff: one screen with the full story. 15. **IL13** - Cost projection accuracy. Financial clarity. 16. **IL17** - Staple handling. Consistency fix. 17. **IL18** - Waste category consistency. Reporting foundation. 18. **IL14** - Multi-event allocation. Power user feature. 19. **IL11** - Package size rounding. Nice-to-have.

title: Spec: OpenCLAW Data Completeness Engine
summary: - `openclaw-mission-control.md` - Real-time Pi dashboard
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.updated: 2026-03-30 (v2 - added scaling data, enrichment analytics, 5 new intelligence jobs)
metadata.what: Archive every scraped flyer as a timestamped snapshot. After a year, this becomes the most accurate historical price database for New England groceries (and eventually nationally).
metadata.theProblem: - Pi systemd services don't set `TZ`. The Pi's system clock might be UTC while the developer is in Eastern time.
metadata.theFix: 1. Add `Environment=TZ=America/New_York` to every Pi systemd service file
metadata.filesToModifyPiSide): - Every `.mjs` file in `~/openclaw-prices/` (add TZ at top)
metadata.whyThisIsPhaseA: Every other phase depends on having a populated canonical database. The polish job needs it for matching. Nutrition lookups need it for local data. Coverage health needs it to know what "100%" looks like.
metadata.steps: 1. Run `node scripts/import-usda-sr-legacy.mjs` (already written, 646 lines, never executed)
metadata.database: No new migrations. `system_ingredients` table and USDA columns already exist (migration `20260401000137`).
metadata.why: Historical data powers sale cycle detection (Phase H), trend forecasting (Phase J), and seasonal analysis. Currently flyers are scraped and overwritten. Archiving them costs negligible storage (text data, not images) and unlocks long-term intelligence.
metadata.rateLimiting: Process in batches of 50. 500ms delay between batches. Non-blocking (failures logged, never thrown).
metadata.whereItApplies: `resolve-price.ts` computes `decayedConfidence` and returns it alongside the existing `confidence` field. Consumers can use either. The existing `confidence` field stays unchanged (backward compatible). New field: `effectiveConfidence`.
metadata.auth: `requireChef()` + admin check for full report. Non-admin chefs see only their own ingredient coverage (tenant-scoped).
metadata.chefPreference: Stored in `chefs` table (new column: `preferred_region text DEFAULT 'haverhill-ma'`). Frontend reads this and passes to all store/price queries.
metadata.scraperParameterization: All scraper scripts accept `--region <id>` or `--zip <code>` as CLI args. No defaults. If no region is passed, the script exits with an error message telling you to specify one. This forces explicit geography on every run.
metadata.geographicPriceInterpolationFutureNotThi: When a chef's region has no data for an ingredient, fall back to: adjacent regions -> state average -> national average. This requires multiple regions to be populated first, so it's out of scope for this spec but the region-config infrastructure enables it.
metadata.howItWorks: After Phase C computes volatility bands, the polish job generates a `refresh-priorities.json` file that the Pi cron can read. High-volatility ingredients get scraped daily. Low-volatility get scraped monthly. The Pi's cron schedule adapts.
metadata.piSideImplementation: The Pi reads `refresh-priorities.json` and prioritizes high-volatility ingredients in its scrape queue. This is a Pi-side change, not a ChefFlow migration. The JSON file is pushed to the Pi via the existing sync API (`POST /api/config/refresh-priorities`).
metadata.idempotent: All inserts use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or ON CONFLICT UPDATE.
metadata.layout: Replace hardcoded dropdown with dynamic region list from `region-config.ts`. Show chef's preferred region as default. Add "Set as default" button.
metadata.states: - **Loading:** Skeleton card with pulsing stat boxes
metadata.phaseAUSDAImport): - Run USDA import. Verify `SELECT count(*) FROM system_ingredients` returns 6,000+
metadata.phaseBPolishJob): - Run polish job in dry-run mode. Verify it reports correct counts without writing
metadata.phaseCConfidenceDecayVolatility): - Check a 90-day-old price in resolve-price output. Verify `effectiveConfidence` < `confidence`
metadata.phaseDCoverageHealth): - Navigate to dashboard as admin. Verify coverage health widget loads with real data, no fake zeros
metadata.phaseEGeographic): - Change region in store browser. Verify store list updates to new region
metadata.phaseFAdaptiveRefresh): - Verify `refresh-priorities.json` is generated after polish job with correct volatility bands
metadata.phaseGScaling): - Verify `SELECT count(*) FROM ingredient_portions` returns 10,000+ after USDA portion import
metadata.phaseHSaleCycles): - Run sale cycle detection. Verify ingredients with 3+ historical price drops get predictions
metadata.phaseISubstitutesPackageOptimization): - Query `getSubstitutes` for an expensive ingredient. Verify 3 cheaper alternatives returned with price difference %
metadata.phaseJTrendForecasting): - Verify ingredients with 5+ price data points have `price_forecast_direction` populated
metadata.phaseKFlyerArchive): - Verify `SELECT count(*) FROM openclaw.flyer_archive` grows after each flyer sync

title: OpenClaw Food Price Intelligence System
classificationReason: Rejected by ChefFlow AI recipe boundary.

metadata.dependsOn: none (builds on existing OpenClaw infrastructure + ChefFlow costing engine)
metadata.estimatedComplexity: large (new Pi services + ChefFlow integration + data pipeline)
metadata.whatThisGivesUs: The national and regional baseline. "The average price of a gallon of whole milk in the Northeast is $4.29." This is the BLS ground truth. When we can't get a local store price, we fall back to this. It's also the inflation and trend tracking layer: "milk is up 3.2% year-over-year in the Northeast.
metadata.scrapingProtocolPerStore: 1. Launch headless Chromium (one instance at a time on Pi)
metadata.instacartScrapingProtocol: - Scrape storefront pages (publicly accessible, no login required)
metadata.instacartFragilityNote: Instacart actively fights scraping. Their HTML structure changes frequently. This layer WILL break periodically. The spec must treat it as best-effort, not reliable. When it's down, the system falls back to weekly flyer data + government baselines for affected stores.
metadata.flyerScrapeProtocol: - Run every Wednesday (primary publish day for most chains)
metadata.wholesaleScrapingProtocol: - Login with developer's credentials (stored in Pi's `.env`, never in code)
metadata.automatedFarmsWithOnlineStores): - Farms using Shopify, Square Online, or Harvie platform
metadata.semiAutomatedEmailCatalogSources): - Farms, fish markets, butchers, local distributors that send PDF price lists
metadata.goal: Prove the concept works with real data.
metadata.discoverySources: 1. Google Maps API (or Google Places) - search for grocery stores, farms, fish markets, butchers, food distributors by region
metadata.discoveryProcessPerRegion: 1. Query each discovery source for food-related businesses in the region
metadata.discoveryCompletionCriteria: When all 6 New England states have been fully scanned across all discovery sources and the registry stabilizes (no new sources found in 3 consecutive scans of a region).
metadata.expectedRegistrySize: 2,000-5,000 sources across 6 states (most will be `scrape_method: "none"` - they exist in the directory but don't have scrapable pricing).
metadata.estimatedDailyPiWorkload: 4-6 hours active (scraping + normalization + processing), remaining time idle or background tasks (trend computation, data aging).
metadata.adaptiveScheduling: If a source's prices haven't changed in 3 consecutive scrapes, extend its interval (e.g., 4 days -> 7 days). If prices start changing again, tighten the interval back. This optimizes Pi resources for volatile sources.
metadata.process: 1. Re-query discovery sources for each region (lighter than initial discovery - looking for deltas)
metadata.runtime: 1-2 hours every 2 weeks. Lightweight compared to initial discovery.
metadata.behaviorOnScrape: - If product exists and price is THE SAME: update `last_confirmed_at` only. No new record. No change log entry.
metadata.learningLoop: Every manual correction and every model-confirmed mapping gets added to the rule-based lookup table. Over time, the rule-based layer handles 90%+ and the model is barely needed.
metadata.defaultForNewSources: `draft_queue`. Developer promotes to `auto_send` after first successful exchange. Major retail chains are always `blocked` (never email Hannaford corporate asking for prices).
metadata.incomingEmailVolume: unlimited processing. No rate limit on reading.
metadata.thermalPaperWarning: Receipts older than 18 months may be partially or fully faded. Check a sample before batch processing. Faded receipts produce OCR errors and should be skipped.
metadata.whatSyncs: - `current_prices.db` - the latest price for every product at every source (~150MB)
metadata.whatDoesNOTSyncToChefFlow: - Change log (historical data stays on Pi for admin dashboard/trends)
metadata.chefPreferenceIntegration: During setup (or in settings), the chef selects:
metadata.features: - Search by ingredient name across all sources
metadata.build: - Data aging pipeline (compress old change log to summaries)
metadata.successCriteria: After 30+ days of data, trend charts show meaningful patterns. Adaptive scheduling has reduced scrape volume by 20%+ without missing changes. Anomaly detector correctly flags test injections.
metadata.whatThisProves: The scraping works, the normalization works, the data is real. Green light to continue.
metadata.migrationSafety: All changes are additive (new columns, new table). No drops, no modifications to existing columns. Standard migration rules per CLAUDE.md.

title: OpenClaw Intelligence Layer for ChefFlow
classificationReason: Rejected by ChefFlow AI recipe boundary.

metadata.scope: All users benefit. No admin-only features unless noted.
metadata.dependsOn: Gemma 4 E2B on Pi (deployed 2026-04-18), existing sync infrastructure
metadata.what: Surface Pi status in ChefFlow admin dashboard. One glance to see if the data engine is healthy.
metadata.nonBlocking: Price enrichment is a side effect. If Pi is down, ingredient still saves. Price appears later on next sync.
metadata.benefit: Developer sees everything in one place. Prevents silent failures like the 46GB WAL.
metadata.dataSource: New Pi endpoint needed: `GET /api/price-patterns/:canonical_id` returning monthly average prices over available history.
metadata.currentState: - Recipe detail page (`recipe-detail-client.tsx`): full pricing display with cost status dots (green/amber/red), cost per portion, "Verified/Partial/Approximate" badge, `CostingWarningList` for missing/stale prices. Uses `last_price_cents`, `last_price_source`, `price_trend_direction`, `last_price_confidence`.
metadata.gap: Pre-event estimated food cost uses recipe `computed_cost_cents` which depends on `ensureIngredientHasPrice()`. If ingredients were created via import paths (brain dump, photo, CSV) before the QW1/QW8 fixes, they have no prices, and the estimate is $0 for those items. The fix in this session (auto-enrich on all import paths) closes this gap going forward. Existing unpriced ingredients from old imports remain at $0 until next sync.
metadata.chefFlowSide: - Upload UI in price catalog or settings
metadata.problemToday: Cross-match uses string similarity. "Heavy cream" matches "Heavy Cream 36% Fat 1qt" but "double cream" doesn't. "EVOO" doesn't match "Extra Virgin Olive Oil". Only 128/69K match.
metadata.constraint: No residential proxy cost yet. Start with regions covered by existing scraping. Expand as justified by chef count per region.
metadata.enhancement: - New webhook type: `price_alert` - targeted to specific chefs based on their ingredient registry
metadata.requires: Per-chef ingredient registry (3.2) so Pi knows which chefs care about which ingredients.
metadata.scale: Average chef uses 200-500 unique ingredients. 100 chefs = 50K registry entries. Trivial for SQLite.
metadata.phase2DashboardIntelligence): 4. "What's cheap this week" digest (1.2) 5. Store recommendation engine (1.4) 6. Push model for price alerts (3.1)
metadata.phase3AIEnrichment): 7. NL ingredient matching via Gemma 4 (2.1) 8. Price anomaly explanations (2.2) 9. Substitution intelligence (2.3)
metadata.phase4Operational): 10. Seasonal price patterns (1.5) 11. Receipt understanding (2.4) 12. Vendor price list parsing (2.5) 13. Geographic expansion (3.3) 14. Unified Pi health dashboard (3.4)
metadata.whatRemyCANNOTDoButShould): - "Is butter cheaper this week?" - No sale calendar access
metadata.cohesionGap: Remy talks about food cost percentage and margins but has no ingredient-level price awareness. If a chef asks "why did my food cost go up?", Remy can say "your food cost is 38%" but cannot say "butter went from $3.99 to $5.49 at Market Basket last week.
metadata.fixSpecItemToAdd): Inject price context into Remy's system prompt via `route-prompt-utils.ts`. When building chef context, include:
metadata.cohesionStatus: GOOD. Dashboard surfaces price data to all users. No email templates reference ingredient costs (email is for client communication, not price intel).
metadata.whatWorksForALLUsers: - Price intelligence summary widget on dashboard (drops, spikes, freshness)
metadata.whatSAdminOnly: - Nothing is actually admin-gated. `OpenClawLiveAlerts` has `isAdmin` check but renders for all.
metadata.oneFinding: `OpenClawLiveAlertsSection` at `dashboard/page.tsx:303-306` checks `isAdmin` but renders `<OpenClawLiveAlerts />` regardless (the check result isn't used as a gate). This means ALL users get price anomaly toasts. Per spec: "All users benefit" - this is actually correct behavior, but the code comment is misleading.

title: Spec: OpenClaw Inventory Evolution (Price Sampler to Full Catalog)
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.status: verified (Phases 0, 1, 5 - ChefFlow + pull service; Pi-side Phases 2-4 pending)
metadata.note: Phases 2-3 (Pi store locator + catalog scrapers) require Pi deployment. Phase 4 (category classifier) is embedded in pull service. Display layer shows proper empty states until data arrives. Migration timestamp adjusted to 20260401000119 (118 was used by prospect source constraint).
metadata.oLD: Search Instacart for a specific ingredient name, get price back.
metadata.nEW: Walk Instacart's department/category tree for a given store, capture every product.
metadata.newApproach: When resolving a price for an ingredient, also check `openclaw.store_products` via the bridge view. If a product name matches the ingredient and a store is within the chef's area, that price can feed into the resolution chain at the appropriate tier.
metadata.scrape: Pi scraper walks store catalog -> writes to Pi SQLite (products, store_products, scrape_runs)
metadata.pull: PC pull script fetches Pi SQLite via HTTP -> parses with better-sqlite3 -> bulk upserts to PostgreSQL openclaw schema
metadata.display: Chef enters zip on `/prices` -> server action queries `openclaw.stores` with Haversine -> returns nearby stores -> chef clicks store -> server action queries `openclaw.store_products` joined to `openclaw.products` -> returns paginated product list
metadata.resolution: Price resolution chain queries `openclaw.store_products` via bridge view when resolving ingredient prices

title: OpenClaw Nationwide Pricing Strategy
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.goal: Map every place in America where you can buy food.
metadata.whyThisMatters: Collection covers ~80% of prices. The remaining ~20% are at stores we can't scrape, products we haven't matched, or locations we haven't reached yet. Derived prices fill those gaps using math applied to facts we already have. No AI, no guessing, no hallucination. Every derived price is labeled as derived, tagged with its method, and scored by confidence.
metadata.sources: - CME Group (delayed quotes, free on website)
metadata.cardinalRule: A derived price is NEVER displayed as a collected price. ChefFlow must always show the source: "Collected" or "Derived (method, confidence%)". A chef must be able to tell the difference at a glance.
metadata.what: Flipp circular data confirms the advertised sale price.
metadata.logic: This is technically collected data (from Category 3), but it only covers sale items on specific weeks. When a circular price is available, it overrides any derivation for that product at that store during that sale period.
metadata.inputsRequired: - Flipp circular match for the product at the target store
metadata.accuracy: 99%+ (it's the advertised price). But only available for items currently on promotion.
metadata.whenToUse: Always check circulars first before deriving. A circular price is effectively a collected price.
metadata.whenItFails: MSRP is outdated, or the product is a store brand with no MSRP.
metadata.minimumDataThreshold: 50 shared products to generate an index. Below that, skip this method.
metadata.stalenessLimit: Do not apply beyond 90 days. After 90 days, the price is marked stale and removed from active display.
metadata.minimumRequirement: At least 2 independent methods must agree within 15% of each other. If methods diverge more than 15%, flag as low-confidence and present the range instead of a point estimate.
metadata.hardFloor: If no method produces 70%+ confidence, the price is NOT displayed. "No data" is always better than a bad estimate.

title: OpenClaw Opus Distillation Burst
summary: **Status:** Tasks 1-2 EXECUTED (2026-04-05), Tasks 3-5 pending
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.costCap: $50 hard limit (actual cost: $0 - executed within Claude Code session)
metadata.concept: Run Claude Opus temporarily to build high-quality durable artifacts in SQLite, then revert to Qwen for ongoing operations.
metadata.method: SQL analytics across 61K anomalies + 65K price changes + 245K current prices.
metadata.whatOpusDoes: Generate explicit variant mappings: "chicken breast" = "boneless skinless chicken breast" = "chicken breast fillet" = "poultry breast". Build a comprehensive synonym table.
metadata.output: Populated ingredient_variants table with verified equivalences.
metadata.durability: Permanent. Cross-match checks variants before fuzzy matching.
metadata.important: This does NOT require running Opus locally on the Pi. The burst can run via Anthropic API from the PC, writing results directly to Pi's SQLite via SSH. This avoids any Pi resource constraints.

title: Spec: OpenClaw V2 - Unified Price Intelligence
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.problem: 8% coverage grows slowly because only Flipp is actively scraping. Five Puppeteer scrapers are built but not scheduled.
metadata.whatTheNormalizerDoes: 1. Takes raw price (cents, unit, quantity, package_size) from a scraper
metadata.backfill: Run once after deployment. Re-normalize all existing prices.
metadata.pREREQUISITE: Phase 2 must be deployed to the Pi before this phase runs. The enriched endpoint depends on `normalized_price_cents` being populated.
metadata.migrationNotes: - Builder MUST run `glob database/migrations/*.sql` first to verify `20260401000108` is the highest
metadata.dedupStrategyForReSync: The partial unique index `idx_iph_openclaw_dedup` on `(ingredient_id, tenant_id, source, purchase_date) WHERE source LIKE 'openclaw_%'` enforces uniqueness at the database level. The upsert query uses `ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` to atomically handle both cases:
metadata.solution: After the sync completes, POST the `notFound` ingredient names back to the Pi. The Pi adds them to `canonical_ingredients` as unpriced entries. Then:
metadata.whyThisMattersForCompleteness: Over time, the catalog grows to include everything chefs actually cook with, not just USDA reference foods. The Instacart bulk scraper then picks up pricing for these new entries automatically. This is the feedback loop that makes the catalog self-improving.
metadata.whyExactSourceMatchMatters: Every query above uses `source = 'openclaw_flyer'` (exact string match, uses `idx_iph_source_lookup` index). The old approach of `source = 'openclaw' AND notes LIKE '%flyer%'` required a sequential scan of every OpenClaw row. With granular sources, the batch resolver can do one query:
metadata.nOTInTheResolutionChainIntentionallyExcl: - API quotes (Spoonacular/Kroger/MealMe) are NOT queried in real-time by `resolvePrice()`. They're too slow (500ms+ per API call) and rate-limited. They remain available for event-level batch quotes via `runGroceryPriceQuote()` in `pricing-actions.ts`, which is a separate use case (quoting a full event grocery list). The resolution chain only uses data already in the local database.
metadata.cachingStrategy: `resolvePrice()` has NO built-in cache. Callers that need caching (e.g., recipe cost page) should use `unstable_cache` with tag `ingredient-prices` (busted by the nightly sync via `revalidateTag('ingredient-prices')`).
metadata.performance: The batch version (`resolvePricesBatch`) does 2 queries total:
metadata.nOTModifiedIntentionally): - `lib/ingredients/pricing.ts` - Nobody imports from this file. Leave it alone.
metadata.policyDecisionRequired: Flipp product names like "Springvale 100% Grass Fed Ribeye Steak" are public retail data. They contain zero client information. Using a cloud model (Claude Haiku) for normalization does not violate the private-data-stays-local rule. But the developer must explicitly approve this.
metadata.implementationNotesForPiEnvironment: - The Pi runs plain Node.js (.mjs files), NOT Next.js
metadata.howItWorks: 1. After rule-based normalization runs, collect all unmatched items
metadata.fallbackOrder: Rule-based (free, instant) -> Cached mapping (free, instant) -> Cloud model (cheap, accurate) -> Local model (free, slow) -> Auto-slug (guaranteed, needs cross-matcher)
metadata.implementation: Create a shared component `components/pricing/price-badge.tsx` that accepts a `ResolvedPrice` and renders the badge. Use this in all three modified components.

metadata.important: The middleware runs in Next.js Edge Runtime. `AsyncLocalStorage` is available in Edge Runtime as of Node 18+, but the middleware callback is synchronous (no long-lived async context). The `x-request-id` header set on the request is what server components and server actions read via `headers()`. The `AsyncLocalStorage` approach is a secondary mechanism for code that runs in the Node.js runtime (server actions, API routes).
