title: Inquiry-to-Revenue Pipeline Interrogation Protocol
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.purpose: 40 high-leverage questions that expose every failure point in the core business pipeline: inquiry intake, event lifecycle, quoting, payment, post-event follow-up. Each question either proves the pipeline works end-to-end or reveals exactly where money, leads, or trust leaks out. Ordered by revenue impact.
metadata.scope: The entire journey from "someone wants to hire a chef" to "chef gets paid and client comes back." Both sides of the transaction: chef and client.
metadata.principle: Every question is binary pass/fail. "Usually works" is not a passing answer. If the pipeline can silently lose a lead, misquote a price, or strand a payment, that is a failure.
metadata.question: Can the chef see their conversion funnel: inquiries -> proposals -> accepted -> paid -> completed?
metadata.currentState: - Public chef inquiry: 8/5min per IP, 4/hr per email
metadata.whyItMatters: This is a Zero Hallucination violation. The client sees a working payment form, enters their card, clicks pay, and gets an error. The UI lied about what was possible.
metadata.passCriteria: - Dashboard or analytics page shows funnel counts
metadata.verification: Read rate limit configuration in each intake surface. Document discrepancies.
metadata.whatToBuild: This is solved by PL2 (expanding the server guard). After PL2, both guards will match: `accepted, paid, confirmed, in_progress`.

title: Spec: Platform Intelligence Hub
summary: Research input:
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.goal: Give the chef a single place to see EVERYTHING ChefFlow captured from their inbox, categorized with surgical precision. The raw feed is the ultimate proof: "We see every email. Here's what we did with each one." Spam isn't hidden; it's showcased (tucked away) as evidence of thoroughness. Scam booking attempts are flagged before the chef wastes time. And a whole new class of email (sponsorship, partnerships, collaborations) gets its own home instead of being lost or misclassified.
metadata.dataSources: - `inquiries` table: counts, channels, statuses, first_response_at (from Phase 3)
metadata.onTheDashboardAddACompactReputationWidge: - One-line summary of all platforms with ratings
metadata.spendEntryOnROIDashboardPage): - "Log Platform Spend" button at top of page
metadata.alsoAccessibleFromSettingsIntegrations: each platform card shows "Total spent: $X" with a "Log spend" shortcut link.
metadata.newSubCategoriesStoredAlongsideThePrimar: | Primary Category | Sub-Category          | What It Catches                                                                                                                  |
metadata.whySubCategoriesInsteadOfNewPrimaryCateg: Backward compatibility. Every piece of code that reads `classification` from `gmail_sync_log` continues working. The `sub_category` is additive. The unified feed uses it for smart grouping; everything else ignores it.
metadata.scoringThreshold: - scam_score >= 4: Auto-classify as `spam` / `scam_booking`, do NOT create inquiry. Show in quarantine with "Suspected scam" badge.
metadata.criticalRule: Scam detection NEVER blocks a real inquiry silently. If scam_score >= 4, the email still appears in the unified feed and quarantine. The chef can always reclassify it as a real inquiry with one click. False positives are recoverable; false negatives waste hours.
metadata.whereOpportunitiesAppear: - NOT in the inquiry pipeline (they're not bookings)
metadata.architecture: The feed reads from `gmail_sync_log` (already stores every processed email) and enriches each entry with:
metadata.keyUXDecisions: - Default view is "All" with most recent first
metadata.scamDetectionOnPlatformEmails: Platform emails bypass `classifyEmail()` entirely (fast-path in `sync.ts:248`). Scam scoring MUST run as a post-parse step inside `processMessage()`, after the dedicated parser returns but before `handleInquiry()`/`handleGenericNewLead()` is called. This is the only way to catch scams arriving via platform sender domains.
metadata.opportunityDetectionAndFoodRelatedness: The `is_food_related` flag from Ollama is NOT available at Layer 4.6. The opportunity detector must include its own food-related keyword check (chef, cooking, food, culinary, dining, catering, kitchen, menu, restaurant, recipe) as part of its scoring signals. Do not depend on the classification output's `is_food_related` field.
metadata.reclassificationCreatesMinimalInquiries: When `convertToInquiry(id)` is called from the unified feed, create a minimal inquiry record using only the stored `gmail_sync_log` metadata (from_address as client email, subject, synced_at as first_contact_at). The chef fills in the rest. Do NOT attempt to re-fetch or re-parse the email from Gmail API.
metadata.backfillStrategyForExistingData: Existing `gmail_sync_log` rows with NULL `sub_category` appear in the "All" tab only. Category-specific tabs (Opportunities, Spam & Scams, etc.) only show rows WITH a sub_category. This is correct behavior (old emails were processed before sub-classification existed). No backfill migration required.
metadata.navPlacement: The `/email-feed` route gets a nav item under the "Inquiries" nav group in `nav-config.tsx`, labeled "Email Feed" with an Inbox icon. Not a standalone top-level item.
metadata.scamBookings: - "Fake inquiry: 'I need a chef for 500 guests, will pay $50,000 by wire transfer' - flagged: unrealistic budget, payment method upfront, generic description, no specific date" (red badge)
metadata.vendorSolicitations: - "Hi Chef, we'd love to supply your kitchen with premium olive oil..." (gray badge)
metadata.aggregatorSpam: - "New lead from CateringRequest.com: Someone in your area needs catering!" (orange badge)
metadata.platformMarketing: - "Your TakeAChef profile was viewed 47 times this week!" (blue badge)

title: Spec: Wire Projected Labor/Travel/Overhead into Pricing Intelligence Pipeline
summary: **Priority:** P0 -- this is the single change that turns ChefFlow from a retrospective ledger into a pre-event pricing tool.
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.priority: P0 -- this is the single change that turns ChefFlow from a retrospective ledger into a pre-event pricing tool.
metadata.problem: `getEventPricingIntelligence()` in `lib/finance/event-pricing-intelligence-actions.ts` hardcodes projected labor, travel, and overhead to `0` (lines 607-609). Line 507 sets `projectedTotalCostCents = projectedFoodCostCents`. This means pre-event margins are always overstated because they ignore 3 of 4 cost layers. A margin-obsessed chef sees 70% projected margin, but the real margin after labor/travel/overhead is 45%. The system lies before the event and tells the truth after.
metadata.solution: Compute projected labor, travel, and overhead from data that ALREADY EXISTS in the database. No new tables. No new UI. No new migrations. The `getTruePlateCost()` function in `lib/pricing/plate-cost-actions.ts` already does this exact computation -- we replicate its data-gathering logic inside the pricing intelligence pipeline.

title: Recipe Costing Integrity - Build Spec
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.scope: Backend computation fixes + UI wiring. No schema migrations. No new pages. Existing architecture extended, not duplicated.
metadata.constraint: The `knowledge.ts` layer already contains operator targets, Q-factor defaults, archetype mapping, and warning types. The `food-cost-calculator.ts` already has operator-aware `getFoodCostRating()` and `getFoodCostBadgeColor()`. The `generate-warnings.ts` already accepts `operatorType`. All of this is built. The problem is the UI pages ignore it. This spec wires existing systems together.
metadata.problem: Two normalizers with different alias sets. `fl oz`, `dl`, `mg`, `sprig`, `slice`, `stick`, `package`, `bag`, `bottle`, `jar` recognized by conversion-engine but not by grocery normalizer.
metadata.fix: In `lib/grocery/unit-conversion.ts`, replace the local `normalizeUnit` with a re-export:
metadata.edgeCases: - Child recipe has `total_cost_cents = null` (uncosted): skip it, do not add 0. This matches existing behavior where unpriced ingredients are already skipped.
metadata.validation: `normalizeUnit('fl oz')` returns `'fl_oz'` in both import paths.
metadata.note: One level up is sufficient because parent's `refreshRecipeTotalCost` now reads child's stored `total_cost_cents` (from fix 1A). Deep chains settle on the next propagation cycle.
metadata.important: Store Q-factor-inclusive cost. The Q-factor is not optional; it represents real costs (oil, salt, wrap) that every recipe incurs. Storing it inclusive means all downstream consumers (plate cost, menu cost, food cost %) automatically get the correct number without needing separate Q-factor logic.
metadata.edgeCase: `chef_preferences` row doesn't exist: `getTargetsForArchetype(null)` falls back to `private_chef` (7% Q-factor). Safe default.
metadata.currentCodeLines197211): Each lifecycle item already has `unit` (from `ri.unit`) and `lastPriceCents` (from `ingredient.last_price_cents`). But we need the ingredient's `price_unit` to do conversion.
metadata.dataNeeded: Add `price_unit`, `default_unit`, `weight_to_volume_ratio` to the ingredient select in the lifecycle data assembly. Store as `priceUnit` and `density` on the item.

title: Restaurant Adoption Interrogation
summary: **Purpose:** Expose every failure point when a restaurant (not private chef) adopts ChefFlow as their primary business platform. Each question targets a real architectural gap, maps to existing code, and forces a binary pass/fail answer. Questions are ordered by blast radius: foundational model breaks first, then operational gaps, then polish.
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.purpose: Expose every failure point when a restaurant (not private chef) adopts ChefFlow as their primary business platform. Each question targets a real architectural gap, maps to existing code, and forces a binary pass/fail answer. Questions are ordered by blast radius: foundational model breaks first, then operational gaps, then polish.
metadata.scope: Owner-operator restaurants (10-60 seats, 2-15 staff). NOT institutional/chain. These operators share the same "one person doing everything" pain as private chefs but operate continuous daily service instead of event-based bookings.
metadata.principle: ChefFlow already has ~60% of what restaurants need (inventory, commerce, staff, stations, HACCP, plate cost). This interrogation finds the 40% that breaks, bends, or lies when a restaurant tries to use it for real.
metadata.question: Can ChefFlow generate a compliance packet (temp logs, HACCP plan, corrective actions, staff training records, allergen procedures) for a health department inspection on zero notice?
metadata.currentState: HACCP system exists. Temp log system likely exists within HACCP. Staff training not verified. Allergen tracking per client exists (dietary restrictions).
metadata.impact: Health inspectors arrive unannounced. A restaurant that can pull 90 days of temp logs, their HACCP plan, corrective action log, and allergen procedures in 5 minutes scores well. One that scrambles through paper binders scores poorly.
metadata.buildPath: Verify: (1) one-click compliance export exists, (2) includes HACCP plan, temp logs, corrective actions, (3) date-range selectable, (4) PDF or printable format. If scattered: build a "Compliance Packet" export that bundles everything.
metadata.passCriteria: Inspector at door. Owner taps "Export Compliance Packet" on phone. Selects last 90 days. PDF generates in under 30 seconds with: HACCP plan, daily temp logs, corrective actions taken, allergen matrix, staff food safety certifications.
metadata.phase1FoundationP0): RQ1, RQ2, RQ6, RQ11, RQ4, RQ18, RQ24
metadata.phase2DailyOperationsP1): RQ3, RQ5, RQ7, RQ8, RQ9, RQ10, RQ13, RQ14, RQ17, RQ22, RQ23, RQ26, RQ27, RQ28, RQ29
metadata.phase3PolishP2): RQ12, RQ15, RQ16, RQ20, RQ21, RQ25, RQ30
metadata.sprint2Changes: RQ5 PARTIAL->PASS (inventory depletion already wired in checkout), RQ28 PARTIAL->PASS (86 toggle action + UI built), RQ29 FAIL->PASS (full tip management: entries, pool configs, 3 distribution methods, register auto-import).
metadata.sprint3Changes: RQ2 PARTIAL->PASS (archetype-aware hero metrics: restaurant dashboard shows today's revenue, staff on clock, MTD, open checks instead of event-centric metrics), RQ4 PARTIAL->PASS (product cost sync engine: cascadeIngredientPriceChange propagates through recipes to product_projections.cost_cents, flags events for cost review), RQ30 PARTIAL->PASS (health inspection export: HACCP plan + 90-day temp logs + corrective actions + allergen procedures + certifications + waste log + equipment in one packet).
metadata.sharedDependency: All 4 need register sales to carry cost data. The inventory bridge (`sale -> product -> recipe -> ingredients`) exists. Need to ensure `costCents` on products updates when recipe costs change, and that this flows into daily aggregation.

title: Scheduled Jobs & Automation Integrity Interrogation
summary: **Purpose:** Expose every failure point in ChefFlow's 29 scheduled cron routes. These jobs run unsupervised - if one silently fails, double-fires, or OOMs, the system degrades invisibly. A broken cron = missed emails, duplicate notifications, stale data, or server crashes with no alert.
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.purpose: Expose every failure point in ChefFlow's 29 scheduled cron routes. These jobs run unsupervised - if one silently fails, double-fires, or OOMs, the system degrades invisibly. A broken cron = missed emails, duplicate notifications, stale data, or server crashes with no alert.
metadata.scope: All 29 routes in `app/api/scheduled/*/route.ts`. Covers auth gates, monitoring, failure recording, idempotency, batch limits, and tenant scoping.
metadata.principle: Every automated job must be authenticated, monitored, bounded, idempotent, and visible when broken. "It runs" is not enough. "It runs correctly, once, within limits, and screams when it fails" is the bar.

classificationReason: Blocked by hard-stop language or source status.

metadata.purpose: Expose every untested failure domain across ChefFlow. Prior 22 specs (674 questions) covered feature-level behavior. This spec covers **systemic properties** that affect all users: data lifecycle, concurrency, recovery, first-contact experience, notification delivery, scale, data invariants, and integration resilience.
metadata.scopeRule: Every question benefits ALL user types (chef, client, guest, public) unless explicitly marked with a role tag.
metadata.verdictFormat: PASS / PARTIAL (with gap) / FAIL (with evidence) / UNTESTED
metadata.passCriteria: Service worker (PWA) serves cached pages. Mutations fail with "you're offline, changes will sync when connected" (or at minimum: "no internet connection"). No silent data loss.
metadata.whyItMatters: Chefs must never feel locked in. If they leave, they take everything. This is table stakes for trust.

metadata.scope: auth boundaries, embed widget gaps, pricing silent-zero bugs, rate limiting, data loss during conversion
metadata.currentBehavior: `compute_menu_cost_cents()` wraps result in `COALESCE(SUM(compute_recipe_cost_cents(...)), 0)`. If recipe cost is NULL (after G1 fix), the NULL is ignored by SUM. Menu cost shows sum of only the priced recipes.
metadata.impact: After fixing G1, a menu with 3 recipes (2 fully priced, 1 with gaps) shows cost of only the 2 priced recipes. Understated.
metadata.buildPath: After G1, update `compute_menu_cost_cents()` to return NULL if ANY recipe returns NULL. Ensures cost incompleteness cascades visibly.

summary: Each question tagged with failure type, current behavior, gap, and build path.

metadata.scope: webhook crash bugs, refund sign mismatch, email delivery gaps, automation loop protection, recipe data pipeline
metadata.currentBehavior: Uses `WITH RECURSIVE ... UNION ALL` with no depth limit. Cycle prevention trigger exists but if data is corrupted (manual SQL, restored backup), CTE could hit PostgreSQL's max_recursion_depth.
metadata.impact: Corrupt data produces wrong cost silently (truncated at recursion limit). No error raised.
metadata.buildPath: Add `depth` counter to CTE with `WHERE depth < 20`. Or change `UNION ALL` to `UNION`.

The design forces cancellation + re-creation for post-confirmation date changes.

title: System Integrity Question Set: Full Cohesiveness Synthesis
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.purpose: Expose every unbuilt bridge between existing working systems. Prior question sets tested domains individually or across 2-3 boundaries. This set tests the connections that SHOULD exist but DON'T, where System A and System B both work but have no bridge. Every gap here means a user does manual work the system could automate, or sees incomplete data when complete data exists elsewhere.
metadata.origin: Synthesized from 4 parallel research agents (backlog miner, question set miner, surface mapper, orphan finder) plus direct codebase exploration. Cross-referenced with 535+ MemPalace conversations, 60+ existing question sets, project-map, and product blueprint.
metadata.scope: 80 questions across 13 domains. Every question targets a real cohesiveness failure that affects ALL users unless marked [ADMIN-ONLY] or [SPECIFIC]. Ordered by leverage: highest-impact gaps first.
metadata.howToUse: Answer each question with CONNECTED (bridge exists, cite file:line), PARTIAL (data exists but pipeline incomplete), DISCONNECTED (both systems work, no bridge), or N/A. Every DISCONNECTED gets a gap number (CS-G1, CS-G2...).
metadata.relationshipToPriorSets: This is the SYNTHESIS layer. Prior sets found domain-specific issues. This set finds the spaces BETWEEN domains that no single-domain audit would catch. If a question overlaps a prior set, it's because the prior set found it within a domain but didn't trace the bridge to the other side.
metadata.cONNECTED4Of5Items): - Thank-you email: immediate via `EventCompletedEmail` + 3-day follow-up via Inngest (`lib/events/transitions.ts:911-993`, `lib/jobs/post-event-jobs.ts:109-115`)
metadata.dISCONNECTEDCSG1): - Tip link: `createTipRequest()` exists in `lib/finance/tip-actions.ts` with full Stripe integration and tokenized public page at `/tip/[token]`, but is entirely manual. Zero reference to tip creation in transitions or post-event jobs.
metadata.dISCONNECTEDCSG2): - "Respond Next" queue (`app/(chef)/inquiries/page.tsx:294-371`) does NOT show booking scores or conflict indicators
metadata.dISCONNECTEDCSG48SectionsSilentlyDegrade: - PriorityQueue: `safe()` fallback sets `allCaughtUp: true`; renders green "All caught up" banner on DB failure (`page.tsx:219-229`)
metadata.dISCONNECTEDCSG5): - Shopping list `completeList()` only updates list status; creates zero expenses/ledger entries (`lib/grocery/smart-list-actions.ts:845`)
metadata.dISCONNECTEDCSG6): - iCal calendar feed (`app/api/feeds/calendar/[token]/route.ts:42-59`) does NOT filter `deleted_at IS NOT NULL`; soft-deleted drafts still appear in calendar subscribers' feeds
metadata.topActionableGaps: 1. **CS-G3 (Waitlist):** Add `getWaitlistStats()` call to dashboard. Data + component already exist. Estimated: 30 min.
metadata.topActionableP1Gaps: 1. **CS-G16 (Circle date change):** Wire `updateEvent` date-change block to query `hub_group_events` -> `hub_group_members` -> send circle notification. Pattern exists in collaborator notification block. ~2 hours.

title: System Integrity Question Set: End-to-End Data Chains
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.purpose: Every prior question set tested WITHIN domains (75Q core, 128Q prep, 83Q network, 70Q financials, 82Q circles, 130Q full-cohesion) or across 2-3 boundaries (31Q cross-system). Real failures happen across 5-6 hop chains where no single domain owner catches the break. This set traces data from ORIGIN to FINAL CONSUMER across every system it touches. A chain PASSES only if the data arrives intact, current, and actionable at the end.
metadata.whatMakesThisDifferent: Prior sets ask "does system X work?" This set asks "when data enters at point A, does it arrive correctly at points B, C, D, E, and F?" One broken hop = entire chain FAIL.
metadata.scoring: Each chain question gets PASS (data flows unbroken from origin to all consumers), PARTIAL (data reaches some consumers but not all), FAIL (chain is broken at one or more hops), or N/A. Every answer MUST trace the full hop path with `file:line` citations at each hop.

title: System Integrity Question Set: Operational Pressure Test
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.purpose: Test whether the system survives real-world chef operations without corrupting state, losing data, or displaying wrong information. Existing question sets test data flow at rest. This set tests temporal resilience: what happens when data changes AFTER downstream systems have already consumed it.
metadata.method: Each question describes a real scenario a busy private chef encounters weekly. Named systems, concrete PASS criteria, business-impact justification.
metadata.scope: Only features that exist today. No aspirational surfaces.
metadata.systems: Recipe sharing, deep copy, concurrent writes
metadata.scenario: Chef A shares a recipe. Chef B and Chef C both accept the share at the same time. Deep copy runs for both.
metadata.pASS: (a) Both get independent copies, (b) no shared IDs, (c) no partial copies (one chef gets full recipe, other gets half), (d) original unchanged.
metadata.why: Network recipe sharing must be safe under concurrency. A corrupted deep copy means a chef has a broken recipe they can't use.

title: System Integrity Question Set: Prep Timeline Real-World Chaos Layer
summary: **Scope:** What breaks when real chefs use this in real kitchens. Edge cases, state mutations, timezone bugs, scaling failures, recovery paths, and cross-user impact.
classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.scope: What breaks when real chefs use this in real kitchens. Edge cases, state mutations, timezone bugs, scaling failures, recovery paths, and cross-user impact.
metadata.prerequisite: PT1-PT25 (core) and PI1-PI20 (integration) must pass first.
metadata.principle: A feature that works in demos breaks in production. This layer tests the messy reality: chefs with phones in wet hands, events that change, menus that shift, timezones that lie, and workflows that loop.
metadata.hypothesis: The checkbox tap target on `PrepItemRow` is large enough for use with wet hands in a kitchen (minimum 44x44px per WCAG).
metadata.impact: Chef in kitchen, hands wet, tapping frantically. Misses the tiny checkbox. Taps 3 times. Toggles on-off-on. Frustrating.
metadata.currentState: Checkbox is `w-5 h-5` = 20x20px (1.25rem). The clickable button wrapping it has the same dimensions. Well below the 44px minimum.
metadata.fixRequired: Add validation: if `safety_hours_max < peak_hours_min`, return an error: "Safety ceiling (Xh) is shorter than earliest peak time (Yh). This recipe cannot reach peak quality within its safety window.
metadata.whoBenefits: Every chef not in UTC. Which is all of them.
metadata.failure: Chef checks "make stock" at 8am. Staff member opens the same event at 10am, sees stock already checked. Assumes it's done. Nobody makes stock.
metadata.verification: Feed the 21 components through `computePrepTimeline` with a service datetime of April 16 6:30pm. Verify no component that should be prepped ahead is placed on day-of.
metadata.risk: The summary bar at the top of the prep tab shows total prep time. If many items use the 30-min fallback, the total is misleading ("4h 30min total" when the real total is unknown).
metadata.failureScenarios: - Chef checks off "make vinaigrette." Removes vinaigrette from menu. Checked state persists forever in localStorage (memory leak).
metadata.fixMinimal): When summing prep time, distinguish estimated vs actual. Or show "~4h 30min" with the tilde indicating some times are estimated.
metadata.fix: Increase the button to `w-8 h-8` (32px) minimum, ideally `w-11 h-11` (44px). Keep the visual checkbox at `w-5 h-5` but expand the clickable area. Can use `p-2` on the button to pad the hit area without changing visual size.
metadata.fixIfNeeded): Store checked items as a JSON set per event (`cf-prep-{eventId} = ["id1","id2"]`) instead of individual keys. One read instead of scanning all.
metadata.question: Is this correct? A draft event's menu might be incomplete. Showing a partial timeline could be misleading, or it could be useful for planning.
metadata.recommendation: Not a V1 problem. Document as a future enhancement: per-season peak window overrides.
metadata.mitigation: Show a warning on the prep tab when no serve time is set: "No service time set. Timeline assumes 6pm. Set a serve time in event details for accuracy.
metadata.result: Correct behavior. The algorithm handles 0/0 correctly. PASSES.
metadata.example: Chef sets peak_hours_min=48 (peaks at 2 days), safety_hours_max=24 (expires at 1 day). The recipe literally cannot reach peak quality before it expires.
metadata.expectedOutput: 1. Components with braised/slow-cook peak windows (short ribs: 24-48h) placed on day-2 or day-3
metadata.gap: Allergen dots are pure color (red circles). A color-blind chef cannot distinguish them from other dots. The leaf icon is used for BOTH "fresh/short window" (emerald) and "vegetarian" (green) - same shape, similar colors. Impossible to distinguish for deuteranopia.
metadata.recommendedFix: Use `date-fns-tz` to construct the service datetime in the chef's timezone:

title: System Integrity Question Set: Prep Timeline Integration Layer
summary: **Scope:** Cross-system integration between recipe peak windows, prep block engine, shopping lists, notifications, client signals, Remy AI, and public profiles.
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.scope: Cross-system integration between recipe peak windows, prep block engine, shopping lists, notifications, client signals, Remy AI, and public profiles.
metadata.prerequisite: All PT1-PT25 questions from `system-integrity-question-set-prep-timeline.md` must pass first.
metadata.principle: The prep timeline feature is only as good as its connections to the rest of ChefFlow. An isolated feature that doesn't feed into auto-scheduling, notifications, shopping lists, or the client experience is a toy, not a tool.
metadata.hypothesis: The prep timeline (including localStorage checkboxes) works fully offline in PWA mode. Chef in kitchen with spotty WiFi can view and check off prep items.
metadata.failure: Three events on Friday, Saturday, Sunday. All have components peaking at day-1. Tuesday through Thursday are packed. Chef doesn't realize until Tuesday morning that they have 12 hours of prep across 3 events.
metadata.verification: 1. Navigate to calendar week view after event confirmation
metadata.currentState: localStorage checkboxes work offline. But the timeline DATA requires a server call (`getEventPrepTimeline`). If the page was loaded while online, it works. If the chef tries to load it offline, it fails.
metadata.fixRequired: Enhance or create a widget that aggregates prep blocks across all upcoming events and flags days with >8h of scheduled prep.
metadata.fixRequiredMinimal): Add a step or indicator to the `EventJourneyStepper` between "Confirmed" and "Event Day" that says something like "Your chef is preparing your menu for peak quality" when prep blocks exist. No operational details, no timing, no recipes. Just a confidence signal.
metadata.impact: This is a food quality failure (not safety for braised, but safety for room_temp designation). A braised protein stored at room_temp for 2h is fine, but the category default pushes chefs AWAY from the optimal 24-48h fridge window.
metadata.mitigationNoSchemaChangeNeeded): The category default is a FALLBACK. When a chef sets explicit peak windows on a recipe, the defaults don't matter. But for chefs who rely on defaults, the `protein` category is misleading.
metadata.options: 1. Split `protein` into subcategories in defaults (not in enum - too disruptive)
metadata.recommendation: If a recipe's peak window says it must be prepped ahead (peak_hours_min > 0), the component should be treated as make-ahead regardless of the `is_make_ahead` flag. The peak window is a stronger signal than a manual checkbox.
metadata.question: When a recipe has peak_hours_min > 0 (must be prepped ahead), but the component isn't marked `is_make_ahead`, which system is right?
metadata.fixRequiredLowEffort): After menu approval, compute and cache the prep timeline. Show a "Prep preview" section on the event detail page for events in `accepted` or `paid` status, not just `confirmed`.
metadata.fixMinimal): If a chef has >50% of recipes with explicit peak windows, show a "Quality-optimized prep" badge on their public profile. This benefits ALL users: chefs get marketing value, clients get confidence.
metadata.fix: Add a print button that opens a print-optimized view (CSS `@media print`), or a "Copy as text" button that generates a plain-text timeline for pasting into Notes/Messages.
metadata.fixLowPriority): When creating a variation, copy peak window fields from the parent. Chef can then override per-variation.
metadata.fixPWAEnhancement): Cache the timeline response in IndexedDB/localStorage when online. Serve cached version when offline. Checkboxes already work offline.
metadata.immediateHighLeverageLowEffort): 1. **PI10** - Fix protein category default (conservative: 0-48h fridge) + nudge when no explicit windows
metadata.nearTermMediumEffortHighValueForALLUsers: 4. **PI4** - Shopping window widget respects grocery deadline 5. **PI8** - Prep deadline notifications (grocery + prep deadlines) 6. **PI9** - Client quality confidence signal in journey stepper 7. **PI18** - Multi-event prep collision detection
metadata.futureLargerScope): 8. **PI6** - Sub-recipe peak window cascade 9. **PI7** - Remy deterministic timeline 10. **PI15** - Public profile quality badge

classificationReason: The item asks for research, validation, or persona evidence before build.

metadata.whyItMatters: Power users live in the command palette. Missing workflows = friction = abandonment.

title: System Integrity Question Set
summary: **Purpose:** Define the complete set of verifiable questions that, when all answered YES, put ChefFlow in a fully specified, trustworthy state. Each question maps to an automated test. Every new feature must extend this set.
classificationReason: Rejected by ChefFlow AI recipe boundary.

metadata.purpose: Define the complete set of verifiable questions that, when all answered YES, put ChefFlow in a fully specified, trustworthy state. Each question maps to an automated test. Every new feature must extend this set.
metadata.principle: A question must be answerable with a binary pass/fail. "Mostly works" is not a passing answer. If the system is ambiguous, the question is not yet specified enough.
metadata.hypothesis: Guest photo uploads and hub media uploads validate file MIME type against an allowlist. Only image types (JPEG, PNG, HEIC, HEIF, WebP) are accepted. File extension is derived from MIME type, not filename.
metadata.failure: Arbitrary file types (.exe, .html, .js) uploaded by unauthenticated users.
metadata.scope: `lib/guest-photos/actions.ts`, `lib/hub/media-actions.ts`.
metadata.testApproach: Structural check for notification block in handleTacClientMessage; email type classification coverage.

title: System Interrogation Protocol
classificationReason: Protects trust, money, safety, completion, pricing, or the governed builder lane.

metadata.purpose: 25 high-leverage questions that expose every unverified assumption in ChefFlow. Each question either proves the system works or reveals exactly where it doesn't. Ordered by blast radius (financial/security first, UX last).
metadata.howToUse: Run `npm run test:system-integrity` to execute all automated checks. Questions without test files require manual audit (marked below).
metadata.existingCoverage: Q1-Q80 (80 specs). This protocol adds Q81-Q100 targeting gaps the first 80 don't reach.
metadata.question: Does every page that fetches data show a loading state while the fetch is in progress, and does that loading state eventually resolve (no infinite spinners)?
metadata.whyItMatters: Without error boundaries, a single component crash blanks the entire page. The chef sees nothing, can't navigate, doesn't know what happened.
metadata.knownSurface: 250+ files return `{ success: true }`. Need to verify each one is preceded by an actual database write (insert/update/delete) that succeeded.
metadata.verification: For every `async` page component and every `useEffect` that fetches data, verify: (a) a loading state is shown during fetch, (b) the loading state resolves on success AND on error, (c) there is no code path where the loading state can persist indefinitely.
metadata.passCriteria: Zero pages that show blank content during data fetch. Zero infinite loading states.
metadata.status: Extend experiential test 08 if gaps found.

title: ChefFlow System Stress Questions
classificationReason: Rejected by ChefFlow AI recipe boundary.

metadata.purpose: 100 high-leverage questions that expose every real failure point.
metadata.secondRun: 2026-04-15 - All P0 fixed, 10 new tests added, 24/24 green
metadata.question: What tier is returned when `trialEnd` exactly equals `new Date()`?
metadata.why: Off-by-one at boundary. `>` vs `>=` determines if trial end moment is trial or free.
metadata.howToTest: Set trial_end to exact current time. Check resolved tier. Verify consistent with intent.
metadata.fix: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` with multi-row VALUES.

title: Ticketed Events & Multi-Channel Distribution
classificationReason: Useful scope, but not required for the V1 operating loop.

metadata.drivenBy: Real farm dinner co-hosting scenario (validated business need)
metadata.principle: Anything a chef promotes gets full distributor backing. ChefFlow publishes once, syndicates everywhere.
metadata.scope: ALL chefs benefit. Not co-host-specific unless marked.
metadata.decision: A ticket is NOT a product projection (POS). It is a new entity tied to events.
metadata.why: POS products are retail items sold at a register. Tickets are capacity-limited, event-bound, guest-linked purchases with dietary collection and circle auto-join. Different lifecycle, different concerns.
metadata.capacityEnforcement: `UPDATE event_ticket_types SET sold_count = sold_count + :qty WHERE id = :id AND sold_count + :qty <= capacity` - atomic, prevents overselling. If no ticket types defined, enforce against `events.guest_count`.
metadata.sEOSocial: Page includes Open Graph meta (title, description, image, date), JSON-LD Event structured data for Google. When shared on Instagram/Facebook, shows rich preview with event image, name, date, price range.
metadata.location: Event detail page, "Distribution" section within Tickets tab
metadata.ticketCreation: Chef creates event -> adds ticket types (name, price, capacity) -> enables "Public Tickets" toggle on event share settings -> share link appears.
metadata.onlineCapacity: Enforced by `sold_count` CAS guard on `event_ticket_types`. Two simultaneous buyers = one gets the last ticket, one gets "Sold Out.
metadata.walkIns: Chef/co-host uses event detail page "Add Walk-In" button. Creates ticket with `source: 'walkin'`, `payment_status: 'paid'` (cash/card at door). Updates sold_count. Optional: POS checkout for payment (bridges to existing commerce system).
metadata.noShowTracking: After event, chef can mark tickets as `attended` / `no_show` for future guest analytics.
metadata.auth: Facebook Page token (chef connects Facebook Page in Settings)
metadata.sync: When chef updates event details (date, menu, description), auto-push changes to Eventbrite if `auto_sync` is true.
metadata.pullFlow: Facebook doesn't webhook ticket sales (it doesn't sell tickets). The Facebook listing is purely a marketing channel that drives traffic to ChefFlow's ticket page.
metadata.mechanism: Groupon requires a merchant agreement. Not API-first.
metadata.enhancement: Submit event sitemap via Google Search Console for faster indexing.
metadata.future: If chef connects Instagram Business account (via Facebook Page), auto-post event announcements.
metadata.whatChefFlowCanDo: Generate a Groupon-compatible deal listing (description, terms, pricing), and provide a redemption flow (Groupon voucher code -> ChefFlow ticket creation).
metadata.problem: Venue partner exists in partner system. Circle admin exists in hub system. No bridge.
metadata.solution: When a chef adds a partner as event collaborator (co_host role), auto-create or match a hub_guest_profile for the partner, add them to the event's circle as `admin` role.
metadata.implementation: Extend the existing pinned notes feature with a new note type: `ingredient_list`. Rendered as a checklist with items, quantities, and "in season" / "limited" / "abundant" tags. Only circle admins can edit. All members can view.
metadata.whatItIs: A shared checklist in the circle where the venue partner posts what's available. Chef references it when building the menu.

title: Token Efficiency Interrogation: 40-Question Developer Cost Audit
classificationReason: Blocked by hard-stop language or source status.

metadata.purpose: Every token spent on mechanical work is money burned. This spec identifies where the developer pays for things that should be free, and builds the infrastructure to stop the bleeding. Every question here directly reduces the $200/mo bill.
metadata.corePrinciple: Claude should only be paid for judgment. Greps, compliance checks, boilerplate docs, context loading, and file re-discovery are mechanical - they should run as scripts, hooks, or pre-computed summaries at zero token cost.
metadata.verdictFormat: PASS (already free) / WASTE (costs tokens, should be free) / PARTIAL (partially automated) / FIXED (was WASTE, fixed this session)
metadata.passCriteria: Every rule that can be mechanically verified is enforced by a hook. CLAUDE.md only contains rules that require judgment.

title: Spec: Visual Representation Strategy (Images Across ChefFlow)
classificationReason: The source artifact already marks this work complete.

metadata.deferred: Phase 3D (culinary_terms table missing), Phase 4B (dashboard too compact)
metadata.problem: `referral_partners.cover_image_url` column exists but most partners probably have no image uploaded.
metadata.solution: - Partner list: show 48x48 cover image or logo (or initials fallback)
metadata.queryGuidance: Event photos are in `event_photos` table (private bucket, requires signed URLs via storage). Dish photos are in `dishes.photo_url` (public bucket, direct URL). Priority: event gallery photo first, dish photo fallback.
metadata.nOTE: The original migration (000121) targeted `equipment_inventory` which does not exist. The correct table is `equipment_items` (`types/database.ts:15668`). A fix migration (000122) has been applied.
metadata.cartridgeScope: - Source: Google Places Photos API, Yelp API, business websites (meta og:image), Facebook pages
metadata.implementation: A static TypeScript map is simpler than a DB table for 39 entries:
metadata.bLOCKED: The `culinary_terms` table does not exist in production. The culinary board page (`/culinary-board`) is experimental and only exists in agent worktrees, not in the deployed app. This phase cannot be built until the culinary board ships as a production feature. Revisit when that happens.
metadata.cONCERN: The schedule widget (`dashboard/_sections/schedule-cards.tsx:44-160`) is already compact: time, occasion, client, guests, weather. Adding images may violate the non-invasive rule. The widget shows 1-3 events and is information-dense.
metadata.solutionIfApprovedAfterDesignReview): - Today's Schedule widget: show a 48x48 dish photo from the event's menu (first dish with a photo)
metadata.alreadyDonePhase1A1E): - Migration applied: `image_url` on ingredients, `avatar_url` on clients, `photo_url` on staff_members, `logo_url` on vendors, `photo_url` on equipment_inventory (wrapped in IF EXISTS)
metadata.phase1BExpenseThumbnails): Page loads -> `getExpenses()` query includes `receipt_photo_url` -> expense table renders thumbnail column -> if `receipt_photo_url` exists, call `getReceiptUrl()` for signed URL -> render 40x40 thumbnail -> click opens detail page with full receipt view
metadata.phase1CEventPhotos): Page loads -> `getEvents()` needs new join: LEFT JOIN `event_photos` (first by display_order) OR subquery for first dish photo via menus -> dishes -> render 48x48 thumbnail in event row -> no photo = existing status dot behavior
metadata.phase1DMenuDishPhotos): Page loads -> `getMenus()` needs new subquery: first dish with non-null `photo_url` per menu -> `MenuCard` renders dish photo as 200px hero banner -> no dish photo = existing card design unchanged
metadata.phase25UploadSurfaces): New server actions for client avatar, staff photo, vendor logo, equipment photo, ingredient photo uploads. Each follows `uploadChefLogo()` pattern. New storage buckets created on demand.
metadata.phase3ADirectoryImages): Pi scraper runs nightly -> sources images (og:image, favicon, optionally Google Places) -> stores locally -> sync cron fires every 6h -> ChefFlow handler fetches from `http://10.0.0.177:8085/api/images/unsynced` -> downloads images to `./storage/directory-images/` -> updates `directory_listings.photo_urls` -> `revalidatePath('/discover')` -> listing cards auto-show images via existing `photo_urls[0]` display
metadata.phase3BStoreLogos): Static TypeScript map + ~39 PNG files in `public/images/stores/`.
metadata.phase3CCatalogEnrichment): Extends OpenClaw price scraping, no ChefFlow changes.
metadata.phase3DCulinaryBoardPhotos): BLOCKED. `culinary_terms` table does not exist. Culinary board is not in production.
metadata.phase4AIngredientReferenceCard): Enhance `app/(chef)/inventory/ingredients/[id]/page.tsx` with hero image, seasonal info, recipe usage list. Must add `image_url` to explicit column select.
metadata.phase4BDashboardPhotos): Add 48x48 dish photo to `schedule-cards.tsx`. Requires `getTodaysScheduleEnriched()` to include menu dish photo.
metadata.phase4CSeasonalPalettes): Wire ingredient thumbnails into palette component.
metadata.phase4DPartnerCovers): Upload UI on partner edit form. Display on partner list/detail. `cover_image_url` column already exists.
metadata.phase1AIngredientImagesBUILT): User clicks "Find Images" -> `enrichIngredientImages()` -> fetches ingredients where `image_url IS NULL` -> for each, `lookupCatalogImage(name)` hits `http://10.0.0.177:8081/api/ingredients?search={name}&limit=1` -> validates name match -> caches URL in `ingredients.image_url` -> `revalidatePath('/culinary/ingredients')` + `revalidatePath('/recipes/ingredients')` -> UI re-renders with `ImageWithFallback`
metadata.phase2UploadsAnyEntity): User clicks upload on entity detail page -> file picker -> `FormData` sent to server action (e.g., `uploadClientAvatar()`) -> validate MIME type + size -> `sharp()` for EXIF strip -> `storage.upload()` to bucket -> update entity column -> cleanup previous file -> `revalidatePath()` -> UI shows uploaded image
metadata.simplificationOpportunities: - Phase 3D (culinary board) should be **removed** from this spec entirely. The table and page don't exist.
metadata.recommendation: Split into 4 separate builder specs (one per phase) for clearer scope. Phase 1 is ready now. Phase 2-4 each need a focused spec.
metadata.8ThingsABuilderWILLGetWrong: 1. **Ingredient detail page will be missed.** It queries `'id, name, category, unit, last_price_cents'` at `app/(chef)/inventory/ingredients/[id]/page.tsx:33`. Builder must add `image_url` to this explicit select. Every other ingredient query uses `select('*')` and works automatically, which makes this one easy to miss.
metadata.uncertainOn: - **Phase 3D** (culinary board): Remove from spec. Table and page don't exist.
metadata.whatWouldResolveIt: 1. Remove Phase 3D from spec or mark as "deferred until culinary board ships

title: Workspace Density: Adaptive Workspace Configuration
summary: **Status:** Ready to build

metadata.what: `resolveChefShellBudget()` in `lib/interface/surface-governance.ts` reads workspace density to control chrome visibility.
metadata.dONOT: - Modify the existing `resolveChefShellBudget` function (other code may call it directly)
metadata.verify: Set test user density to `'minimal'`. Navigate the app. Confirm: no Remy chat widget, no feedback nudge, no live alerts, no market research banner. Sidebar and mobile nav still visible. Set to `'standard'`. Confirm Remy and all chrome returns.
metadata.layoutRedirectLogicAddToAppChefLayoutTsx: Inside the `ChefLayout` function, AFTER the existing `requireChef()` call and BEFORE the return JSX, add:
metadata.cRITICALRULES: - The `/welcome` page shell budget must hide sidebar and mobile nav (add to `resolveChefShellBudget` like the immersive editor pattern)
metadata.approach: In the `ChefDashboard` function (line ~1484), fetch workspace density alongside the existing parallel data fetches. Then use it to conditionally render sections.
metadata.step1: Add import at top of file:
metadata.step2: Add to the `Promise.all` block at line ~1493:
metadata.step3: Add a boolean for minimal checks:
metadata.step4: Wrap sections in density checks. For `minimal` density, HIDE these sections by wrapping them in `{!isMinimalDensity && (...)}`:
metadata.result: A `minimal` density chef sees: greeting, today's schedule, and quick notes. Three sections. Clean.
metadata.problem: `resolveChefShellBudget()` currently takes only `pathname`. It needs to also know density. But density requires a DB call, which is async, and this function is sync.
metadata.solution: Add a new function `resolveChefShellBudgetWithDensity(pathname, density)` that extends the base function. The layout already does a DB call; it passes density into the new function.

metadata.goal: To prevent critical details (preferences, history, menu decisions) from being lost in the high volume and complexity of multiple simultaneous events and long-term client relationships.
metadata.1CentralizedProfileVisibility: *   The system must provide a single, dedicated "Client Profile" view linked to every client, regardless of the event date.
metadata.2PersistenceAndSearchabilityHistoricalDa: *   The profile must store non-event-specific details (e.g., allergies, dietary preferences, established family routines, common party size ranges, specific equipment requirements).
metadata.3PreEventMandatoryReviewWorkflow: *   The system must incorporate a configurable "Pre-Event Checklist" or "Mandatory Review" reminder tied to each event.
metadata.4MultiDimensionalDataInput: *   The system must accommodate varied party sizes and types (e.g., Family of 4, Corporate party of 50, Intimate dinner for 2, Repeated booking history).
